Review Title: Intelligent Script plus Munnabhai-esquire fun = a sure entertainer
Review Text: Coming from the director of the “Munnabhai” series, on the face of it, 3 idiots is a complete 'paisa vasool' (value for money) comedy. As with his earlier flicks, it has benign intentions at heart, and some lessons for the system. Vis-à-vis a goon do-gooder there, you have an enigmatic genius here who seems turn everything that he touches into gold; which includes smiles, encouragement, healing, love, creativity, tears and some hard lessons.The system under attack here is similar to that in Aamir’s previous masterpiece “Tare Zameen Par”: Education as he steps up his ire from schools to professional colleges that emphasize on cramming and bagging ranks. One of the gags says: “if you don't run, you'll be like a broken anda (egg)”. Facing the attack is the fictional Imperial College of Engineering, modeled on the famed IITs and shot on campus at IIM Bangalore.Drawing inspiration from Chetan Bhagat’s ‘5 point someone’, it tracks the lives of its 3 protagonists Ranchhoddas Chanchad or Rancho (Aamir Khan), Farhan Qureshi (R Madhavan) and Raju Rastogi (Sharman Joshi) through their college days, their battles with the dictatorial Director Viru Sahasrabuddhe or ViruS (Boman Irani) and a dabbles into Rancho’s love interest and ViruS’ daughter and a doctor, Pia (Kareena Kapoor).3 Idiots also tackles issues such as pursuing a stream of education against one’s own creative interests or heart’s calling, teaching a hard lesson to ragging seniors and an odd lesson to slimy nerds. While he goes about things, he gets Pia to fall for him too. Rancho’s bold methods irk ViruS, the similar antagonist from Hirani’s Munnabhai. Of course, as must all movies end, Rancho opens up ViruS’ eyes towards the end.The funniest scene in undoubtedly is the one involving Rancho modifying Chatur’s speech. While it had to praise ViruS for his ‘chamatkar’ (miracles) in the college, the modified ‘balatkar’ (rape) brings out the reality. With Omi Vaidya delivering it to rote perfection, you’d be rolling on the floor laughing. Here, don’t also miss Hirani’s lesson that mugging up without understanding is ‘balatkar’ of knowledge.While it’s not all perfect, there are times when the drama is overdone, especially the delivery scene on a TT table! But considering its other goodies, overlooking this is quite easy. The best goodie comes in the form of editing wherein it weaves the past and present events as Farhan and Raju accompanied by a vengeful Chatur try to unearth the whereabouts of the ‘real’ Rancho who disappeared during the convocation.Aamir Khan is synonymous with quality films. It’s another thing though that I found last year’s "Ghajini” purely average and it turned out to be a hit. But, with 3 Iditots, his sole release for this year, Aamir, in my reckoning is back to his glory days of “Tare Zameen Par”. If the rave reviews and BO collections are to be believed, 3 idiots is a Smash-Hit. If you have still not seen this meaningful laugh riot, rush now for a bite of its 'gyaan' (wisdom).

Review Title: The three idiots show us what is wrong with  our current system of education
Review Text: Aal iz not welleducation systemRajkumar Hirani and Vidhu Vinod Chopra coupled with Aamir Khan have come out with an ingenious film. The film exposes the fact that the Indian Education System needs to be revamped and how well they do it. Picturized in trademark Hirani style, the film is a must watch for every student and parent. The current scenario where teachers feel that whatever has been taught in class is the only thing that the student needs to learn and a scientific or questioning mind is not welcome in the classrooms is shown in good humour. The student too gets conditioned to this rote learning or falling in line syndrome for earning favours from the teachers. The setting is in a premier engineering college of the country ICE where three students are thrown into the situation because their parents wanted them to do so. This is so true of innumerable students who are just following their parents diktat in opting for engineering. Madhavan wants to get into wildlife photography but is forced into ICE. Sharman has to do it because he has to succeed and support his family which is struggling for survival with an ailing father and a working mother and an unmarried sister of marriageable age.Aamir Khan is the odd man out in the trio with a penchant for technology and a sharp and questioning mind. But these threesome are found unfit by the college director (Boman Irani) . In the leadership of Aamir Khan they thwart the college's efforts to punish their waywardness but to no avail . By the time they reach the final year there are two groups – one the studious , loyal types and the other is the threesome who are mischievous and non-conforming. Time and again they pester the college authorities with their pranks .Aamir is not the serious study variety but is successful in topping the exams . This point drives home the issue that if you have the passion you can do it – you don't need to cram.After being rustigated from college, Boman changes his heart and takes them back because their ingenuity saves his daughter as they help her deliver her child in extraordinary circumstances and with the help of Kareena , who is a doctor and also Boman's daughter. Aamir disappears after his college only to be traced by his friends in what is the ultimate goal of his passion. The gags, jokes, ragging scenes, classroom sequences, romantic scenes, gatecrashing into marriages and all such bits in the movie are so realistic that all of us would have done the same in the colleges. Aamir is superb and looks the college student. He fails to surprise because each time he surpasses himself and has the uncanny knack of choosing the right films.Madhavan and Sharman support him to the hilt as true friends who are ready to crashland flights, gag car owners and ditch the college authorities to support each other. The light-heartedness is there but there are some touching family scenes too which makes the movie one of the best in recent times. Raju Hirani proves to be a master storyteller again. Swanand Kirkire's lyrics are youthful and meaningful. Shantanu Moitra's music is adequate but is the only mediocre thing in an otherwise brilliant film.Boman is his usual hilarious with grey shades . Kareena is beautiful .

Review Title: Watch it as one more comedy movie ..
Review Text: Not what I expected from Raju Hirani, at least after 'Lage Raho'. This film is shallow, promising to be, but just not the picture of engineering college or engineers at all. Evidently no one from Director's family ever had been an engineer and neither he had seen one. Ever if you go and watch this film, just watch it as an mindless (supposed to be) hilarious ride, with almost every joke that you may have heard of. Movie loosely based on the spiced book '5 point someone' by Chetan Bhagat, makes you realize that book was cent percent a better read than the movie. Too long, predictable and TOO LOUD on being 'Ever Joyful Enthusiast Loving Caring' personality. Performances to be acknowledged are of 'Boman Irani' playing the Dean of the Institute & of 'Omi Vaidya' playing Chatur Ramalingam aka 'Silencer'. Best and most humorous scenes are filmed with them. In whole I am going with 6/10 for this one because, it did make me laugh out loud in some of the scenes.

Whoa Whoa Whoa ! Just watched this gem movie ,and gotta say ,this is probably the most entertaining movie I've ever seen in My life and probably Yours too. Wont spill out any spoilers here but would of course stamp Excellent Performance!on each and every actor of this movie Bows for Aamir ! Must add I had watched Avatar just yesterday and though it was fascinating it was not as influential and touching as 3 idiots. Theater response was too good , and that dhan-chamatkar scene was simply Way too Hilarious . For those whom it itches the fact of Aamir is playing a college kid, Aamir initially refused to act but later accepted this as a challenge improvised it an crafted role to Perfection. Sharman's scenes were also amazing few were tear jerking while while few were Hilarious , Maddy especially excelled in His Father Son argument scene . Also length wise it is perfect; and choreography , hostel scenes and few emotional scenes were executed with perfection .

Excellent movie. Easily one of my top 5 movies of all times. Superb acting by everyone in the movie...especially Aamir Khan, Boman Irani and Sharman Joshi. I hope they get awards for their performances. Script was excellent with a very important message. And despite its comedy genre, it proves to become an easy classic of bollywood cinema. Songs aren't the greatest but still seem to do OK and fit; movie is greatly focused on the plot. Aamir Khan once again proves to have the touch of gold exceeding his excellence in his film. Just when you think he broke the Bollywood records with Ghajini, he does it again with 3 Idiots by a mildstone. He's truly the greatest actor in bollywood history....and deserves the title of King Khan!!!

Review Title: 3rd time lucky for Hirani with 3 Idiots
Review Text: Rajkumar Hirani has succeeded once again! This film is right up there with, if not over and above, the Munnabhai films. Everyone played their parts exceptionally well in this must see blockbuster comedy. Aamir Khan has now become an actor who chooses his films and roles with perfection. Boman Irani (the only actor who has appeared in all of Rajkumar's films) failed to disappoint once again.There were side-splitting scenes that I will remember for life, but I do not want to give these away. They will have to be seen before they can be believed! With every good story, there is always an underlying and important message. In this film, it is the following: "Every person in this world is born with a gift and a passion, which cannot morally be forced upon that person." An inability by Indian families to recognise this fact of life is leading to tragic consequences, which can tear such families apart.For me, 3 Idiots has made the end of 2009 an ever-lasting memory. May this film win as many awards as possible!

Review Title: aamir once again proves when it comes to perfection no one can beat  him.
Review Text: 3 idiots is the best film to catch this Christmas. And one of the finest film of the year. after lagaan ,rang de basanthi ,fanaa ,tzp ,gajini ,aamir once again has come up with an excellent performance in 3 idiots .3 idiots is personally my favorite aamir film till now. aamir's role as 'rancho' is mind blowing, no one can fit this role as aamir did ,and proves us why he is called perfectionist.madhavan n sharman in supporting roles has helped the film to its expectation .though kareena has little to offer in this film ,but still she steals the limelight.though the film is filled with lot of emotions ,it is over shadowed by the hum our in the film.i think and wish 3 idiots to break all the current box office records and steal as many awards as possible.though this years Indian film has already sent to Oscars ,i feel they should see this film and rethink.overall 3 idiots is complete paisa vasool film.it has an excellent message for youth to offer and shows the dark sides of the current education system. my rating 9.5/10

It was a hilarious comedy movie, explaining about the awful condition of studies in India. Now a days many are just concentrating on marks and not interest , but they don't know that if we have interest we get the subject automatically. It was heart touching even in parts when there are comedy scenes. Music is good in the movie the songs like ALL IS WELL and Zoobi Doobi were good as everyone thought but the other songs like Give me some sunshine were heart touching and also very good in timing during the film. Comedy was reasonable as expected but also the "AW" scenes were also very good for the viewers. A complete family movie and also a movie for the youth and a movie from which our education system can learn.All over because of this i give this film 10/10

You watch 3 Idiots and realize that Aamir has done it again ! It started with Rang De Basanti which instilled the passion of truthfulness to Taare Zameen par which was a message about child-centric creativity, the similar ideas are propounded in 3 Idiots.3 Idiots is a mixture of realism and Bollywood Masala which targets a very important, yet subtle issue of our Indian society. Considering that fact that most of the population in India is young and further that most of them pursue sciences as their degree and compete with a gargantuan mass of peers to stand where they are right now, the story just aims to get some saneness in our society, which the old wise men argue and the younger ones forget. Think and learn things for the love of knowledge, rather than the desire of success. It highlights the essential flaw in our system, that the person studying in IIT's and IIM's is not because of his passion for technology/managing skills but because of his academic abilities.A contemporary theme executed with contemporary brilliance and carefully dramatized masala to keep the audience entertained. If the medium budget films did well last year, mainly because of the realism in them, big budget like this has all the ingredients for a blockbuster. The plot focuses of inventive thinking, engineering design which, I , personally well relate to and share similar opinions. The plot is like any Masala movie, with a protagonist which desires a change in opinions and a climax which shatters the mindset of the pessimist, a love story, a friendship packed with chirps of comic relief. The strong theme just completes the movie and drives the message straight to the eye. Aamir deserves most of the credits for his acting while Vidhu Vinod Chopra and his team have done a commendable job with the sets, locales and editing and lighting. Hats Off ! 10/10P.S. : before watching the movie, i was shocked to hear a German and a Ukranian going bonkers about the movie. such is the brilliance of contemporary bolly cinema.

Review Title: Only stupids will not like "3 Idiots".....
Review Text: Don't know where to start or rather who to start with! It has been 3 years since we had the bombshell called "Lage Raho Munnabhai" from Rajkumar Hirani Factory and had been waiting for another movie which can exceed the previous two movie masterpieces. While we were still waiting for "Munnabhai Chale Amerika", Hirani dropped a nuclear bomb this time.Yes, this movie is no less than a nuclear bomb, in every terms, every aspect of it. Its a simple story about 3 students who refused to give up their dreams against the conventional norms of contemporary Indian Educational System, where marks and the degree certificate are all that is used to judge a student, where individual and unconventional thoughts are not welcome and suppressed every day, every hour, every minute. Its simple, if you do not go along the system, it will break you down to the point where you either lose your identity for the rest of your life....or just quit (read suicide).Yes, this may seem over dramatic to people, but student who suicide after facing the immense pressure of traditional grading system, expectation are not just in fiction. I myself had a friend, who stood before the Rajdhani Express train one day, because his parents expected him to always achieve distinction marks in every exam, which he could never do and on failing every time, his parents lied to everybody they know saying that he achieved 80% in the exams. Imagine the guilt and inferiority complex inside the kid, who didn't try to move away from the tracks when a Superfast Train ran towards him to crush him over.I am not trying to give away the story or post a spoiler, as almost every Indian College student has read Five Point Someone by Chetan Bhagat, which is the base of this movie. Yes, Rajkumar Hirani has modified the story except for some key incidents and inserted the moral messages wherever he could.Result - the film has turned into a laugh-riot and tear-jerker at the same time. You will never feel over-melodramatic in a single scene of the movie, neither will you feel that some buffoonery is going on any of the comedy scenes. This is a movie which will keep poking you every single second with innocent heartfelt emotions and make you laugh, make you cry, make you clap, make you whistle.Coming to the actors, let's not comment on Aamir Khan's capability. He is just brilliant as an Engineering Student who doesn't care about scoring in an exam, but loves acquiring knowledge whichever way he can. R. Madhavan is fantastic and excellent as the narrator of the movie and a friend everybody dreams of. Sherman Joshi rocks with his highly expressive acting as the hesitating student, who keeps stumbling trying to follow the road his other two friends have taken. Kareena is looking very pretty and natural as the unconventionally strong girl who knows how to stand up against the wrong. Boman Irani is funny and brilliant as he smoothly carries the role of a Einstein-lookalike strict College Director, one of whom we have in every colleges in India. Omi Vaidya is the surprise package, who enacts the Hindi-learning highly-competitive student who can make a Parrot ashamed with his ability to mug up.Realizing how big the review is turning, I will sum up all the other small actor's work as "Brilliant"! Hats off to Shantonu Moitra and Swanand Kirkire for meshing the songs in the story so beautifully, that we don't feel them as forced. And salute Rajkumar Hirani for giving us another highly commercial movie which has more dosage of social and moral messages than any of his previous films.So basically, as far as I think, only stupids will not like this movie...and I am not a stupid, I am an Idiot. But that's just me. So go find out, whether you are an "Idiot"! The Idiots rock!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Review Title: A Heartfelt and Hilarious Journey of Friendship and Education
Review Text: 3 Idiots is a joyous and thought-provoking film that takes a critical look at the pressures of the educational system while celebrating the value of friendship and the pursuit of passion. The film follows three engineering students-Rancho (Aamir Khan), Farhan (R. Madhavan), and Raju (Sharman Joshi)-as they navigate their academic struggles, relationships, and personal dreams. Through humor and emotional depth, 3 Idiots explores the importance of following one's heart and challenging societal expectations.The film's charm lies in its balance of laughter and introspection. It cleverly critiques the competitive, rigid nature of traditional education systems, while also delivering powerful messages about creativity, failure, and success. Aamir Khan's performance as Rancho is both charming and inspiring, bringing a sense of optimism and resilience to the character.The screenplay is rich with memorable moments, funny antics, and heartfelt scenes that resonate with viewers, making it a timeless classic. 3 Idiots is an entertaining and meaningful exploration of friendship, self-discovery, and the importance of education that leaves an emotional and lasting impact.

Review Title: It will make you laugh out loud. It will make your eyes moist. It will entertain you full on.
Review Text: Amir Khan is known in Bollywood as Mr. Perfectionist. The movie lives up to his name. Amir does it differently than everybody else. He proved it time and again through movies like Lagaan, Ghajni, Tare Zameen Par etc. And in this movie also, it is Amir on whose shoulders the charm unfolds. This is about three students in India's top engineering college. Amir teaches us through this movie that one can attain all the satiable success and glory in this world as long as he chases his dreams. It is YOUR life so care less about what others have to say and believe in your own heart's voice. At times the movie is emotionally intense, for instance, when Raju (Sharman) tries to commit suicide, and it is also full of LOL moments, for example, almost all encounters of Rancho (Amir Khan) with the principal aka VIrus(Boman). To conclude, the movie is a complete package, arguably the best movie in the history of modern Bollywood. It earned over 200 crores in India alone, the first ever.

Review Title: Watched it multiple times, still entertaining
Review Text:
I have watched this movie a lot of times and never got bored always enjoyed thoroughly. If we talk about the collections, many Bollywood movies have crossed its grossing though but, they aren't even close to its standards. Getting a 300Cr film in 2009 was not easy. Well, I know one should never judge by the collections but, sometimes when they cross a mark, indicates even the intellect audience is paying to watch it. The narration itself from Farhan's POV keeps you bonded with the movie. All the characters were so strong that you fall in love with them easily. You will only agree with me when you watch it. Simply, Raju Hirani + Aamir Khan = Masterpiece!!!
In one sentence - the best Bollywood movie ever! If you are into movies, then this one is not to be missed. The acting is incredible by the ensemble cast. The script is funny and at the same time. Even the scenery is breath-taking. Although the length of the film is pretty long and has some quirky musical numbers, 3 idiots is a delight.
It is worth the watch! I laughed! And I'm giving this a 10!

This movie really changes the feel of the Indian film. In the past, I felt that Indian films had something very superficial like the scenes in which actors looked directly at the lens (as seen through the 8-year-old bride film), and unnecessary slow motion shots, or as well as the music that takes up most of the movie (Enthiran). But this movie made me really like the Indian film. The music in the movie is really cute and suited to the situation of the film, it honors the film a lot.The film talks about what each person thinks about what the university really does for each person. What are we looking for in college? We learn rote and seek knowledge to get a degree that makes ours get a job with a high salary and then worry about rice rice money things. And here Shadon is different, he's against mechanical thinking, he's looking for a simple explanation for the definitions. He really learns to love the industry, he wants to learn it. He did not pursue knowledge, he did not pursue money, he did not pursue success. Simply everything he does because he wants to know more, because he is curious, he doesn't wants to race with anyone in the race of knowledge, money, or success.I really learned a lot from this movie. I was very touched in the scene that Shadon send his dad's friend to hospital, as well as when the friend suicide, then also when the delivering baby scene. A movie worth watching.

The story of 3 idiots has been told differently and most importantly, the story offers so many twists-n-turns that you just can't guess what would unfold next.There's so much happening in every scene and the screenplay is so gripping that you don't feel the need to look at the auditorium ceiling or at your watch at brief intervals. You aren't restless. 3 idiots demonstrates how strong film-making can enhance and elevate an already solid concept. film is nothing short of an event. The supremely talented actor acts in one film a year and no two films are ever identical in terms of plot line.To sum up, 3 idiots is commercial Hindi cinema at its best. The film has 'Hit' written all over it. Let me put it this way: Cancel whatever you're doing today and go watch 3 idiots instead.Director strikes a fine balance between humor and emotions. The comic portions are executed with panache, the drama is attention-grabbing and the emotional quotient is strong enough to turn you moist-eyed. The marriage of humor and emotions as also technique and content is what drives 3 idiots to the winning post.

I watched "3 Idiots" on a whim, having heard snippets of its cultural impact but not knowing much about the plot or characters. From the moment the film began, I was drawn into the vibrant world of Indian engineering students, where humor and heartache intertwine seamlessly.The film's narrative is a delightful rollercoaster, oscillating between laugh-out-loud moments and poignant life lessons. I found myself chuckling at the hilarious antics of the characters, particularly the unforgettable scenes involving the quirky yet wise Rancho, played by Aamir Khan. His charisma is infectious, and the way he challenges the conventional education system resonates deeply. It's a refreshing reminder that life is not merely about grades and accolades, but about passion and understanding.What struck me most was how relatable the themes were, regardless of cultural background. The struggles of the students, their friendships, and their pursuit of dreams felt universal. I particularly loved the mantra "All is well," which serves as a comforting reminder during tough times. It's a simple phrase, but it encapsulates the essence of resilience and positivity.The cinematography was stunning-each frame bursting with color and energy, perfectly complementing the upbeat soundtrack. The dance sequences, often a hit-or-miss for me in films, were surprisingly engaging and added to the overall joy of the experience.As the movie progressed, I was amazed at how quickly the nearly three-hour runtime flew by. The storytelling was so compelling that I was fully invested in the characters' journeys, eagerly anticipating their next moves. The climax was a masterclass in storytelling, delivering an emotional punch that left me reflecting on my own life choices long after the credits rolled.In the end, "3 Idiots" is not just a film; it's an experience that encourages you to embrace your individuality and chase your dreams with fervor. I walked away with a smile on my face and a renewed sense of purpose, eager to share this gem with friends and family. If you haven't seen it yet, do yourself a favor and dive into this heartwarming tale-you won't regret it!

Review Title: 3 Idiots: A Masterpiece that Leaves You Laughing and Reflecting
Review Text: I recently had the pleasure of watching the Bollywood classic "3 Idiots," and I must say it exceeded all my expectations. From the brilliant storytelling to the outstanding performances, this film is a true gem that seamlessly blends humor, emotion, and a powerful message.The narrative follows the journey of three friends, brilliantly portrayed by Aamir Khan, R. Madhavan, and Sharman Joshi, as they navigate the challenges of the Indian education system and societal expectations. What sets "3 Idiots" apart is its ability to make you burst into laughter while delivering profound insights into the flaws of our education system and the importance of pursuing one's passion.Aamir Khan's portrayal of the unconventional and free-spirited Rancho is nothing short of phenomenal. His charisma and energy light up the screen, making Rancho a character that stays with you long after the credits roll. The chemistry between the three lead actors is palpable, and their camaraderie adds an authentic touch to the narrative.The film's director, Rajkumar Hirani, deserves immense praise for his storytelling prowess. He masterfully weaves together humor, drama, and social commentary, creating a cinematic experience that appeals to a wide audience. The screenplay, penned by Abhijat Joshi and Rajkumar Hirani, is witty, thought-provoking, and beautifully crafted.Beyond its entertainment value, "3 Idiots" addresses serious issues in the education system and societal pressure. The film encourages viewers to question the conventional notions of success and happiness and to pursue their passions fearlessly. It does so with a perfect blend of humor and sincerity, making it a rare film that entertains and enlightens simultaneously.The music by Shantanu Moitra is soulful and complements the narrative beautifully, enhancing the emotional impact of key scenes. The cinematography captures the scenic beauty of Ladakh and other locations, providing a visual treat for the audience.In conclusion, "3 Idiots" is a cinematic masterpiece that not only entertains but also leaves a lasting impact. It is a must-watch for anyone seeking a heartfelt story, outstanding performances, and a refreshing take on the pursuit of happiness. Prepare to laugh, cry, and reflect on life's important lessons - "3 Idiots" delivers it all with charm and brilliance.

Review Title: MY REVIEW: Non-Idiotic Bollywood Feel Good Fun!!
Review Text: 3 Idiots. The greatest director of recent times shows that cinema is magical. He shows, you can club the mainstream masala in a purposeful film flawlessly. All you need is directorial excellence and magical touch of Rajkumar Hirani, supreme performances by as many as six actors in a single movie; Aamir Khan, R Madhwan, Sharman Joshi, Omi Vaidya, Boman Irani and Kareena Kapoor, a story that touches all generations especially youth, a water tight screenplay which feels like a roller coaster ride of fun, emotions, pain, drama, thoughts and new ideas. There are numerous outstanding scenes especially the comic ones. Music might sound a bit below average but in movie it is used intelligently and never holds the fun for you. All in all, it is simply no nonsense Bollywood main stream cinema which serves the masses and classes with equal ease and love.

Review Title: Melodramatic nonsense composed of one liners
Review Text: If you enjoy 40 year olds depicting teenagers delivering cringey one liners at every scene that are designed to make you cry, then this film is you for you.On the other hand, if you haven't suffered severe brain trauma by being battered over the head with a cricket bat, rendering you completely numb to the creative arts, then this film might not be for you.I myself am somewhere in between, and enjoyed the catchy tunes, so 5 stars.

My favorite movie in the year
"3 Idiots" is a Bollywood comedy-drama directed by Rajkumar Hirani, starring Aamir Khan, R. Madhavan, Sharman Joshi, Kareena Kapoor Khan, and Boman Irani. The film revolves around the lives of three engineering students and their journey through college life, friendship, societal pressures, and the pursuit of individual passions.The movie received widespread acclaim for its engaging storyline, powerful message about the education system, and stellar performances by the cast. Aamir Khan's portrayal of the free-spirited and innovative character, Rancho, resonated with audiences, while the chemistry among the lead actors added depth and authenticity to the story."3 Idiots" blends humor, emotion, and social commentary seamlessly, addressing issues such as the pressure of academic expectations, the importance of following one's dreams, and the flaws within the education system. Its thought-provoking narrative, coupled with heartwarming moments, made it a massive success among audiences and critics alike.Overall, "3 Idiots" is not only entertaining but also carries a powerful message, making it a highly impactful and beloved film in Indian cinema.

Review Title: Writing a review on one of my favourite movies named "3 idiots"
Review Text: My name is Sammy and I live in a city called Dhaka which is located in the heart of Bangladesh. I am really big fan of watching different types of movies such as English, Hindi and Bangla. Among of them, "3 idiots", a hindi movie that is my most favourite one that I have ever seen for several times. In the following paragraphs, I would like to write a review on this comedy movie.The name of the movie as represents that there are three major characters who individually played a vital role in each part of the movie. As Amir khan is the main actor of this movie; Madhavan and Sharman were also played outstanding role to make this movie more amusing for the audiences. I strongly relate my life with this movie.For example, Farhan wanted to study for wildlife photography as he was passionate on it, though his father forced him to be an engineer. Here I wanted to be a shooter and I have a wish to wine Olympic gold meddle. But it is matter of fact that my family sometimes encourages me to get married rather taking the shooting as profession. Moreover, Raju wants to be an engineer to end his family's poverty and often got fear of Poverty. I can relate this scenario with one of my friends who couldn't continue her study in a private University just because of poverty. Rancho, on the other hand, is genuinely passionate about science and engineering. His unexpected answers in class made this movie funny as well as educative. To give an example, Rancho witnesses the effects of the school's hyper-competitive academic culture. when a student named Joy Lobo commits suicide after failing to meet a project deadline and being denied graduation by Virus. I think that our education culture in Bangladesh also going through an unexpected sick competition. Every year a few students used to commit suicidal attempt just because they couldn't get A+ in public exams."aal izz well" is an unique line that used in this movie which has also a great impact in my life. This line used by Amir khan when he used to face any difficult situation. Actually, human beings often go through a number of problems in their life. Having problems doesn't mean that life is gone and give up the highest try to achieve the destination. This movie inspired me to try the level best to achieve the goal.To conclude my view regarding this movie, I can say that this is the best movie ever made that every age group of people should watch and should change their view of education, especially the parents group. To add more, this movie also important for the students who get frustrated and give up their try at early stage. The most important and lead character in the movie is Rancho. He is a perfect example of genuine wisdom. Hence, I will recommend the students to watch this movie if they want to be a future leader.

Review Title: Not worth a top 1000 list, Disappointing
Review Text:
After watching this movie for more than 2 long never-ending hours I have to write down the reasons why this movie is not ok:1. Very childish repetitive humour ... How many times do we have to put the pissing scenes ?!!
2. Unrealistic. India streets are not clean and out of traffic like in the motorcycle scene.
3. Too long with empty content.
4. Too many stereotypes.
5. Highly overrated, when you watch it based on IMDB score you expect much much more.
6. Wrong messagesSome persons point out its positive messages !! Well ... I don't think that justifying "achieving your dreams" message should be justified through Bullying, falsifying identities, lack of respect to professors, or recovering after suicide. In fact, I think the messages are very disappointing.Some of the acting is good, the main song is catchy and colours look nice. But I do not see any other positives.It can probably deserve a 4 or 5, but I will just give it a 3 because I really don't understand how can this movie be so high in the top 250.I think the top 250 should give some weight to the credibility of the voters.

The movie's strongest point is its ability to blend comedy with drama seamlessly. The jokes and gags in the film are genuinely funny and add to the lightheartedness of the movie, but the film also deals with serious issues such as the pressure on students to excel academically, the importance of following one's dreams, the importance of being a brave person and the shortcomings of India's education system. The performances by the lead actors are also commendable, and their chemistry on-screen is palpable. Indian culture in this film is thickened by the emergence of typical Indian songs and dances sung by the main cast pair which gives a festive atmosphere typical of Indian films.The film's plot is engaging and keeps the audience hooked from start to finish. There are many unexpected events that are neatly designed, arousing greater curiosity. The movie can make you cry and laugh at the same time, mix up your feelings. The various twists and turns in the story, along with the relatable characters, make the film an enjoyable watch. Each actor has a strong character that makes the film even more alive. The film also boasts of some memorable scenes such as the "All Izz Well" sequence, which has become an iconic moment in Indian cinema.The movie's themes are still relevant today, and it serves as a reminder to chase our dreams and not conform to societal norms. The movie is a classic and a must-watch for anyone who loves good cinema. With all these considerations, this film deserves an 8 out of 10.In conclusion, "3 Idiots" is a must-watch movie that manages to entertain, educate and inspire all at once. Many moral messages that can be obtained. The film is a testament to the fact that cinema can be both commercially successful and socially relevant.

3 Idiots is a 2009 Indian Hindi-language coming-of-age comedy-drama film written, edited and directed by Rajkumar Hirani, co-written by Abhijat Joshi and produced by Vidhu Vinod Chopra. Adapted loosely from Chetan Bhagat's novel Five Point Someone, the film stars Aamir Khan, R. Madhavan and Sharman Joshi in the titular roles, marking their reunion three years after Rang De Basanti (2006), while Kareena Kapoor, Omi Vaidya and Boman Irani appear in pivotal roles. Narrated through two parallel dramas, one in the present and the other set ten years in the past, the story follows the friendship of three students at an Indian engineering college and is a satire about the social pressures under the Indian education system.

3 Idiots directed by Rajkumar Hirani. The story of the film is about India's education system, which does not encourage students to choose their destination by their wish, rather than it forces us to follow the regular rules of the system to race.
In this film, we see a lot of characters Rancho(Aamir Khan), Farhan(R Madhavan), Raju (Sharman Joshi) are the main leading characters. They got admitted to the city's elite college, where they meet their principal Viru Sahastrabuddhe(Boman Irani). Who was very strict with his rules. For that everyone in the college was afraid of him but in behind everyone call him VIRUS.
Rancho was joyful and limitless who always gives challenges to the difficulties of life, takes it very lightly. Raju and Farhan were different. They were afraid of parents, teachers, and also exams. Both of them have their different family problems. In this movie, we see how Rancho changes their life and also their way of thinking. Here we also can see an interesting character Chatur (Omi), Who always follows the race and want to become the winner. Presence of Priya( Kareena Kapoor) makes it quite romantic and emotional. And the small argues with VIRUS entertaining the audience, especially the Chamatkar/Balatkar speech. Music & Choreography were really awesome. By watching this movie I personally enjoyed it a lot, and also inspired too. I will recommend everyone to see this movie at least once in your life.

Review Title: must watch movie with good moral
Review Text: "Life is a race: If you're not fast enough, you'll get trampled," booms engineering college dean Viru Sahastrabudhhe . The stakes are high: in India 2.0, students whose parents have sacrificed everything for their education are expected to reach the top. But three brainy misfits -- Farhan, Raju and Rancho. find a common bond when they realize that their futures aren't exactly inscribed in a mathematics book.Written and directed by Rajkumar Hirani, whose two previous films also tweaked Indian sensibilities with great commercial and critical success, "3 Idiots" has dominated at the box office holiday season. In India, the Reliance Big Pictures release collected more than 1 billion rupees in its first four days of release.A great part of the film's appeal is Aamir Khan, who has come to specialize in heroic iconoclasts, such as a rebellious villager in "Lagaan" or a visionary teacher of special-needs kids in "Taare Zameen Par." As Rancho, a young engineer of dazzling inventiveness, Khan conveys smarts, mischief and, finally, compassion, as he spurs his friends on to greatness before he mysteriously disappears.

This movie is a masterpiece, for so many different reasons. I have seen this movie many many times. There was a period of time where I would watch this movie, or at least parts of the movie, multiple times in the week. Yet, it never got old. I still cried at the sad scenes and laughed just as much at the happy parts. This is because the events in the movie are just so touching and can cause anyone watching to connect with the characters. The movie deals with so many issues, such as the dichotomy between career and passion and the stresses and mental health of college students. It holds so many beautiful lessons while discussing these issues, such as what it means to have a true friendship, the love of learning, and the importance of resilience and optimism in life. The main character makes this movie what it is, and is such an amazing person that truly inspires me. He has such a passion for learning, is the best friend anyone could ask for, and is simply kind and loves life. He is grateful for life and his experiences in a way that is so inspiring - all he wants is to soak up as much knowledge as possible through life and have a positive attitude. That being said, the other 2 main characters also have wonderful backstories and their character development is so beautiful and inspiring to watch. I can say this is one of my favorite movies, and I will never get bored of it. The countless lessons this movie provides are worth the watch, and the comedy in the movie is very well-written. I struggle to put into words how wonderful this movie is. Enjoy!! :)

This appears to be a brilliant, funny movie. But there's a BIG PROBLEM if you don't speak Hindi. At least my copy has less than 10% of the dialog in subtitles. An entire 4 minute speech, with everyone laughing, had absolutely NO subtitles. Huge parts of the dialog, literally minutes go by with people talking back and forth, apparently with funny, witty dialog, and finally the last sentence just says "come." I am enjoying the movie but missing so much. Not sure if I have an early release or what? Actually, even the dialog that is translated is quite poor...sometimes the wrong word is used.The parts of the movie that I could follow are really charming. The actors have great facial expressions and quirky moves. I love the whole parody of the Indian education process for engineering.

It's hard for me to review this film, as I have not seen a huge number of Indian films--probably no more than a couple dozen. Most of the ones I've seen were wonderfully entertaining but I am far from an expert on Bollywood. Because of this, I have a hard time knowing how good this film is relative to other films from this country. So, consider this when you read this review. This may be among the very best India has to offer or it just seems that way to me.Like so many Indian films, this is a very, very long film--with a run-time of almost three hours. When a movie is bad or just okay, this can seem like forever, but since "3 Idiots" is a very, very good film I loved its length. And, like most films of the genre, it has its share of the usual singing and dancing so foreign to films from other countries. One thing you should know, however, is that defining the type of film it is isn't really easy. Much of it is a comedy, but it also has many poignant moments (keep the Kleenex nearby), some existential moments where they explore the meaning of life and work and it's also a tender film about friendship.

3 Idiots" is more than just a comedy; it is a poignant commentary on the Indian education system and societal expectations. The film critiques the rote learning culture and emphasizes the importance of pursuing one's passion. Rancho's mantra, "All is well," becomes a symbol of optimism and resilience, inspiring viewers to face life's challenges with a positive outlook.3 Idiots" is a timeless classic that combines humor, heart, and a powerful message. With outstanding performances, brilliant direction, and a captivating storyline, it is a must-watch for anyone seeking a film that entertains, educates, and uplifts. Whether you're a student, a parent, or simply a lover of great cinema, "3 Idiots" will leave you with a smile on your face and a renewed perspective on life.

Review Title: An almost 3 hour marathon but worth every minute.
Review Text: From the flow of screenplay is like butter,one thing leads to next, to the past and present scene jumps, the viewer always know where he is in the movie. It is also not a genric collage-life movie as there is no reference of sports like in movies like Student of the Year or Chhichhore. Also the characters written are cliche but convincing. Cinematography is fine, the campus selection is too good as it represents old and strict mindset also represented by Boman Irani's character. It is arguably Hirani's and Aamir's magnum opus. Also the music is good. Making it a emotional joyride masala movie.

I'm from the Philippines. For years, I have always been curious about Indian cinema. I've seen clips of Indian films on cable TV and to be honest...I just don't get it. I don't get all the silly dancing and singing in almost all movies. I find it so corny and irrelevant to the story. But don't get me wrong, Philippine movies back in the 80's were also filled with corny singing and dancing. But we have gone past those and transitioned into movie-making based on good scripts...rather than cheesy story lines.When a friend of mine recommended this movie, I haven't really heard of it. I thought it was a Hollywood release but it wasn't. It was a Bollywood movie. He said it was really funny, so out of curiosity, I got hold of a copy.My first thought was that it was nothing but a silly story of 3 guys doing slapstick comedy that I have all seen before. When they started singing "All iz well.." I told myself... I knew it! Just what I thought.But as the movie progressed...I was amazed at the intelligence embedded in this movie. I was so impressed with the smart dialogues delivered by Amir Khan. It was so mature and thought-provoking. The life crisis all of them were in... the pressures at school..the pressures from society and the pressures from family as well... they rang so true. I was even at one time crying my heart out. I can't believe it! All the preconceived notions about a Bollywood movie were obliterated. I was so moved by this picture and I have no regrets seeing it.

Review Title: 3 Idiots is a masterpiece - heartfelt, humorous, and thought-provoking. A film that entertains, educates, and inspires all at once.
Review Text: 3 Idiots is one of those rare films that perfectly balances entertainment with a meaningful message. The story is brilliantly written - fresh, engaging, and deeply relatable. It explores the pressure of the education system and the importance of following one's passion in a way that feels both emotional and inspiring.The script is sharp and beautifully layered, blending humor, drama, and emotion effortlessly. Every dialogue feels natural yet impactful, with memorable lines that stay with you long after the movie ends. The screenplay is tight and wonderfully paced - it keeps you invested from start to finish, moving smoothly between college fun, emotional moments, and thought-provoking themes.Rajkumar Hirani's direction brings the story alive with warmth and sensitivity. Aamir Khan, R. Madhavan, and Sharman Joshi deliver outstanding performances, while Boman Irani and Kareena Kapoor add depth and charm.

Aamir Khan, R. Madhavan, and Sharman Joshi deliver outstanding performances, while Boman Irani and Kareena Kapoor add depth and charm.

Review Title: Entertaining, Social Message, Art of Living, Creative
Review Text: As usual, Amir Khan once again made a very meaningful movie, named "3 Idiots". This movie being a comedy but educating real art of living a perfect life. All the instances are selected on the basis of giving a message to us that we should live our life meaningfully rather than following the trends blindly.Though,it is difficult to count all the story. However, examples of presence of mind of the character is really appealing, e.g. producing electricity from batteries in the hour of crisis, explaining definition of a machine by rolling his pant zip. We must understand the subject rather than cramming whether it is educational or real application life.Our life is precious. We cannot destroy it under stress just because we cannot fulfill the aspiration of other.Missing ingredient is spirituality which must be inculcated to make a proper base for growth.In nutshell, it's a superb, must watch movie.

"3 Idiots" (2009), directed by Rajkumar Hirani, is a landmark film in Indian cinema, a powerful commentary on the education system and the pursuit of individual passions. It's a film that seamlessly blends humor, emotion, and social commentary, resonating with audiences across generations.The narrative centers around three engineering students at the prestigious Imperial College of Engineering (ICE): Rancho (Aamir Khan), Farhan Qureshi (R. Madhavan), and Raju Rastogi (Sharman Joshi). Rancho, the unconventional and brilliant student, challenges the rigid, rote-learning-based system, advocating for a passion-driven approach to education. Farhan, an aspiring wildlife photographer, and Raju, burdened by family pressures, are initially trapped in the conventional mold, but Rancho's influence inspires them to pursue their true callings.The film cleverly uses humor to highlight the absurdity of the prevailing education system. Rancho's witty remarks and unconventional methods, often clashing with the authoritarian director, Viru Sahastrabuddhe (Boman Irani), provide much of the film's comedic relief. However, beneath the humor lies a serious message about the importance of critical thinking, creativity, and following one's passion."3 Idiots" effectively portrays the pressures faced by students in India, where academic success is often equated with societal validation and financial security. The film exposes the detrimental effects of this pressure, leading to anxiety, depression, and even suicide. Raju's struggle with poverty and his family's expectations, and Farhan's conflict between his passion and his father's wishes, are relatable to many viewers.Rancho's character is the driving force of the film, embodying the ideals of intellectual curiosity and individual freedom. His philosophy of "All is well" serves as a mantra, encouraging his friends to overcome their fears and pursue their dreams. Aamir Khan's performance is exceptional, capturing Rancho's charm, intelligence, and unwavering belief in his principles.The film's emotional core lies in the friendship between the three protagonists. Their bond is a source of strength and support, helping them navigate the challenges of college life and beyond. The film also explores the complex relationship between Rancho and Pia (Kareena Kapoor), Sahastrabuddhe's daughter, adding a romantic element to the narrative."3 Idiots" is not without its critics. Some argue that Rancho's character is idealized and that the film simplifies complex issues. However, its impact on Indian society is undeniable. The film sparked a national debate about the education system and the importance of pursuing one's passions. It inspired many young people to question conventional norms and to follow their dreams.The film's message is universal, resonating with audiences beyond India. It speaks to the human desire for freedom, creativity, and self-discovery. The film provides a powerful message to students, parents, and educators alike.In conclusion, "3 Idiots" is a thought-provoking and entertaining film that has left an indelible mark on Indian cinema. It's a film that encourages us to challenge the status quo, to embrace our passions, and to live a life that is truly fulfilling.

I'm writing to express my deepest appreciation for the film '3 Idiots.' I've lost count of how many times I've seen it, but the number is easily in the hundreds. What amazes me is that despite knowing every scene and every line of dialogue, the emotional impact remains as powerful as the first time.The film's genius lies in its ability to be both hilarious and deeply moving. The characters of Rancho, Farhan, and Raju feel like old friends, and their journey resonates with me on a personal level. It's a story that challenges conventional thinking, celebrates the joy of learning, and reminds us to follow our hearts.The scene where they all reunite at the end, or the moments of quiet friendship, still bring tears to my eyes without fail. It's a testament to the brilliant writing, direction, and performances that the movie's emotional core remains so potent. I truly believe '3 Idiots' is a cinematic masterpiece, and it's a film I will continue to cherish and revisit for the rest of my life.

Review Title: Interesting, entertaining, and teaches many new lessons!
Review Text: Genuinely interesting, entertaining, and teaches many new lessons of life! Especially for students like me. First of all, I have zero interest once I saw the title even posters of the movie. Because I thought I will be bored, similarly to some movies that I had watched before that resemble this movie title. Then my teacher recommends me this movie, so I started to search for the synopsis of this film. My interest appeared little by little and I decided to watched this movie. Exceeded my expectations, this movie succeeds to change my point of view and give me plenty of moral stories that I could apply in my daily life. School, dream, friendship all packaged in an interesting musical comedy that doesn't disguise the moral values to be conveyed. One of the dialogs that memorable for me is when Rancho said, "Never study to be successful, study for self-efficiency. Don't run behind success. Follow behind excellence, success will come all way behind you." Because so far, my goals and dreams have only been successful and I just tried to increase my grades. This movie also teaches us to maintain someone's trust, have big courage, to be sure what we want to do, always helping each other, and never be overbearing to other people. I would recommend this movie to my friends and family because it is worthy to watch and have a really exciting plot!

3 Idiots is a one of the best Bollywood movie. Amir khan is one of the finest actor of Bollywood industry. Who always selected best story and best team for film making. His all film are very creative and give a messages to audience.Chatur Ramalingam, a successful vice-president, reminds his old college rivals Farhan Qureshi and Raju Rastogi about a bet he made with their classmate and Chatur's nemesis Rancho ten years ago. Chatur has returned to India to conclude a business deal with Phunsukh Wangdu, a famous inventor. The three go to Shimla to find Rancho, reminiscing about their time at the Imperial College of Engineering (ICE) in Delhi.In college, Rancho was passionate about learning and often clashed with the strict college president, Dr. Viru Sahastrabuddhe ("Virus"). When a student named Joy Lobo is denied graduation by Virus for not submitting a project, Rancho tries to help but finds Joy has committed suicide by hanging. Rancho confronts Virus about the intense pressure on students, but Virus dismisses him.One night, Rancho, Farhan, and Raju crash a wedding party, not realising it is for Virus's daughter Mona. Mona's younger sister, Pia, is initially upset with Rancho but starts liking him after he exposes her materialistic fiancé Suhas. Pia breaks up with Suhas. Virus warns Farhan and Raju about associating with Rancho, so Raju moves in with Chatur, a competitive student who relies on rote learning. Rancho and Farhan prank Chatur by altering his Hindi speech, leading to his humiliation. Furious, Chatur challenges Rancho to see who is more successful in ten years.Before their final exams, Raju's father has a heart attack. Rancho and Pia help save him. Virus bets Rancho that neither Farhan nor Raju will get a job after graduation. Rancho tells his friends why they struggle: Farhan's passion is photography, not engineering, and Raju lacks confidence. Farhan and Raju promise to confront their issues if Rancho confesses his feelings for Pia. They drunkenly break into Virus's house, and Farhan and Raju urinate on his letterbox. Virus notices Raju at his doorstep and threatens to expel him or to witness to Rancho. Not wanting to disappoint his family or to betray Rancho, Raju attempts suicide but survives, leading Virus to revoke the expulsion. Raju succeeds in the interview and gets a job, and Farhan convinces his father to let him become a photographer.Humiliated at Raju's success, Virus sets a difficult exam to fail Raju so he cannot claim the job. With Pia's help, Rancho and Farhan steal the exam paper, but Raju refuses to cheat and throws it away. They are nonetheless caught and are expelled.Following the trio's expulsion, Pia, having given the office key to the trio, finally confronts her older sister, Mona, and her father by revealing that her brother had actually committed suicide due to the pressure his father had put him through into becoming an engineer, since he wanted to pursue his own dream by becoming a writer instead, but only to simply write a suicide note.On a stormy night, a pregnant Mona goes into labour while unable to reach a hospital, but successfully delivers her baby with Rancho's, Farhan's and Raju's help. In gratitude, Virus forgives the three, passing down his space pen to Rancho as a mark of honor. On graduation day, Rancho disappears.In the present, Farhan, Raju, and Chatur reach Shimla and find out that the real Rancho was a different man. Their friend was actually Chhote, the son of Rancho's family's gardener, who took Rancho's place to get an education. The real Rancho tells them Chhote's address in Ladakh. On the way, they stop Pia's wedding with Suhas in Manali and convince her to join them.In Ladakh, they find a school run by their friend. Chatur mocks Rancho, thinking he is merely a teacher, but is shocked to realize that he is Phunsukh Wangdu, the person Chatur is making a business deal with. Chatur accepts defeat, and the friends run away laughing.

On graduation day, Rancho disappears.In the present, Farhan, Raju, and Chatur reach Shimla and find out that the real Rancho was a different man. Their friend was actually Chhote, the son of Rancho's family's gardener, who took Rancho's place to get an education. The real Rancho tells them Chhote's address in Ladakh. On the way, they stop Pia's wedding with Suhas in Manali and convince her to join them.In Ladakh, they find a school run by their friend. Chatur mocks Rancho, thinking he is merely a teacher, but is shocked to realize that he is Phunsukh Wangdu, the person Chatur is making a business deal with. Chatur accepts defeat, and the friends run away laughing.

3 Idiots is not just a film - it's an emotional journey filled with laughter, wisdom, and soul-stirring moments. From the very first scene, it grabs your attention with its wit and charm, and as the story unfolds, it delivers powerful messages about life, education, friendship, and the courage to follow your own path.The performances are nothing short of brilliant. Aamir Khan delivers one of his most memorable roles, supported by an outstanding cast that brings depth and authenticity to every character. The chemistry between the leads feels natural and deeply moving.What makes this film truly special is its ability to balance humor and emotion so seamlessly. It will make you laugh out loud, reflect deeply, and perhaps even shed a tear - all within the same scene. The direction, writing, and music come together in perfect harmony to create an unforgettable experience.Without revealing any plot details, I can say that 3 Idiots is a film that stays with you long after the credits roll. It's one of those rare movies that entertains while leaving a lasting impact on your perspective toward life and learning.Highly recommended for all ages and backgrounds. If you're looking for a film that touches your heart, makes you think, and lifts your spirit - this is it.

Review Title: The best movie I ever seen
Review Text:
Last Saturday, we watched an Indian movie entitled "3 Idiots". It is a great film and I must tell you that it is a great experience. At first, I actually taught that it is a typical comedy movie but I was wrong. There are so many lessons to be learned in the film. The first lesson is we should always follow our passion. Very often, we deceive ourselves by giving in to the demands of the people around us, rather than following what our minds dictate. We go by the set standards of the society and ignore our real passion.
We end up becoming average professionals in the field that we enter and on the other hand, the field that happens to be our real passion loses a potential talent. An example given in the movie is a person who has a passion for photography walks into an engineering college and ends up becoming an average engineer, while the fine arts industry loses a good photographer. The second lesson is do not learn to get success, learn for knowledge. When we strive hard to learn and improve our knowledge, we move ahead in life.
Never run after success but let it automatically happen to your life. The third lesson is we should enjoy the present. We either lose ourselves in the maze of past success and failures, or worry ourselves sick about the future. We cannot change what has already happened and we cannot predict what happens in future. Instead, we should focus on doing our day to day work with eagerness and excitement, then we end up positively influencing the future.
Helping others helps you grow mentally and easily associates you. A lasting relationship is created by helping others. In the film, Rancho helps Raju and Farhan in choosing right careers for them and follow their dreams. This creates an everlasting bond between friends, not only friends but with anyone whom we help. You grow as a person and never have to look back in life. This movie is an eye opener to each one of us. I recommend that every student should watch this film, they will not only apply it to their studies but to their everyday life.
Aamir Khan as Rancho plays the role of a very bright student who motivates his friends pursue their passion and make a career out of it. R.Madhavan as Farhan who wants to become a wildlife photographer but was forced to pursue engineering because of his strict father . Sharman Joshi as Raju who is pursuing engineering with the goal of getting his family out of extreme poverty. Boman Irani as virus who plays the role of a strict college director who is highly competitive Kareena Kapoor as Pia who plays the role of virus's daughter and falls in love with Aamir khan. The film goes very smooth. The film's first half includes ragging scene, confrontations with the professor, Chatur's speech and mournful meal at Raju's home. Overall the film depicts a very nice mingle of comedy, suspense and motivational. All the actors and co-actors done a commendable job. The music of the film was very soothing. The back ground music supports the film all through the movie. Overall dipiction is fine. Some of the scenes are really emotional. Overall rating is excellent. It is definitely a one-time watch.

Review Title: How to learn with basic values
Review Text: My Views 3 Idiots looks at our educational system which pressurizes the students and does not allow to chose his destination by his own wish, in a satirical way and brings out some of the flaws in it... The intermission point offers a twist that is totally out of the blue and keeps you hooked to the movie ! Just when you think this feel-good comic caper is leading to its culmination, there is another surprising element at the climax which is brilliantly weaved out by Hirani.His looks and mannerisms as a college student was genuine and consummate.. Madhavan and Sharman adds up with commendable performances... Kareena does her part well... Among the supporting, Boman Irani as the eccentric head of the institution was too good.

I'm usually not a fan of Bollywood or Indian films, but i think this movie is the other way around. Such a life-changing movies. With a spice of comedy-drama, this movie raised an issue on mostly of developing countries, how terrible the education system. Mostly people from developing countries, people really take the grades to be the qualification rather you would become successful or not. They don't care rather you understand the subject or not, as long as you get high grades.Rancho, the main character of this show, proven that this system all are a wrong way to go. He captured a point that we shouldn't study to succesfulness. We should study for perfection. And I totally agree with him. Because mostly if you just study for a high grades, after the test or whatsoever, you would forgettin' all of your study. This movie also came up with a sensitive issue on social life, such as suicidal and late marriage.Like the other Indian movies, this movie also have "that" singing and dancing along together. The friendship between Rancho and friends also captured and left a pleasant mood during this show. As cinephile 3 idiots provided the best interpretation by the putting some depth dialogue. I have listed down some of dialogue that said, "Bacha kabil bano, kabil. Kamayabi to sali jhak marke piche bhagegi"
(Purse excellence and success will run after you). No doubt, being capable of something will always lead you forward.This film is a way to go if you want to watch type of movies that will bring you into a burst of laughter and tears.

I was very excited to watch this movie because I thought the concept was a good one but so things just threw me off.I'll start off with the parts I enjoyed. The singing and dancing scenes are some of the best and the message is a good one. The characters are all likable and have reasons behind their actions instead of just doing this of the hell of it. It's also pretty funny but I wouldn't consider this a selling point for the film.Alright now I disliked how dull some parts felt like they are dragging out scenes just to get the screen time in. The plot also feels haphazard and strung together without clear marks to tell me what was going on. Finally it has these weird sound effects randomly throughout that completely threw me the first time it happened. So unnecessary.

I can't tell how much i love this film. As someone who grew up with chinese and hong kong comedy movie, 3 Idiots absolutely pulls me in, the long duration doesn't matter cz u're always laughing with the story, just like Stephen Chow movies. The way they handle their message is amazing, the transition between comedy moments and the deep philosophical moments is flawless, u can be crying about how funny the scene was and 10 seconds later u're crying bcs of the message they trying to tell. In a world where most movies, especially hollywood only tries to tell their message and political agenda, more than the storytelling, it is such a breath of fresh air to rewatch this movie that only tries to tell the message about life and humanity using the comedy and characters, without any political message.

Review Title: Let  Meaningful cinema go to the dogs......this is Good anyway.
Review Text: Before I penned down this review I managed to browse through a few of " Hated It" reviews. To be sure they were absolutely ridiculous ( barring a few honest ones) . I mean I don't have any right to dictate as to who should like which movie but after reading the reviews I felt a little nauseatic because almost all of them were steeped in vindictive propaganda and were not by any means not genuine critical appraisals.O.K. the humour sometimes did touch on the cheap side of things. But let me assure you this movie was in no way any more deplorable or depraved or Low than DEV -D or Kaminey , if that is what these gung-ho critics want to say.A sweet aberration from the novel, the movie has been well made. Individual performances vary like maxima -minima . While Aamir is ordinary , the supporting cast is exceptionally good. Sharman Joshi is a good actor who is being ignored . Omi Vaidya and his dialogues are great. The guy was suited to the role and he performed it well. Boman Irani is always entrusted with difficult parts. I say this because in many movies he has been asked to play the role of a real life eccentric with reel life eccentricities.Kareena is a big disappointment ( always has been ).The music is ordinary . I hate the song Zoobie Doobie . It is monotonous , boring and shabby.In the totality of things a great movie with some great defining moments. Even if you leave real;ism and social messages aside as also Aamir's hyped image , this movie is by all means a remarkable one and deserves to be watched once if not twice or more.

3 idiots is a story of a man who lands from a different planet to our planet earth and finds our culture, religion, traditions and way of living little wierd. He starts asking questions from society and starts to expose some wrondoings happening within our system, particularly in terms of religion. Aamir Khan in his character as PK has really managed to entertain the audience with his funny character and inncocence. ANushka Sharma as a journalist also has lent a fair amount of support. Boman Irani also as a senior journalist did good job. SO was Saurabh SHukla and other cas of the film. Sanjay Dutt as a local Rajasthani did good amount of entertainment to the auidence with his pairing with Aamir Khan character PK. Saurabh Shukla character as a powerful religious god man was authoritative as well as strong and vocal. He did a good job. All actors of the film have done equal amount of contributon to make the film look good. The story, writing , narration and screenplay of the flm was also good and entertaining. It had its own funny moments as well as thought provoking question moments. Some mments will make you qustion some practices followed particular to eligion that exists in our society. I forgot to mention, SUshant SIngh's character was also really good in the film. His performnace in tis film is one of his best acts till date. Sadly, he had a very small role in the film. ANushka and Sushant, Anushak and Aamir chemeistry looked good on screen. Also, i liked the chemistry of Anushka and Boman Irani in the film. It was eally good to watch them on screen together. SOund music, songs and background music of this film be it with the lyrics also top notch in this film. Really need an apprecaition for this. Dance steps of this film were simple at the same time, uniuqe. This film had its own set of emotions. Differnt from contempary movies of that time. I would say PK is a really bold movie, specially when talking about hard facts about religion. Good part about the film is this film teaches us to believe in relgion but not fall into the trap of these fake godmen existing in society who use religion as a means to mint money to make business. I had a great time watching thiss movie. This movie at some parts felt like a MR. BEan movie, specailly looking at the character of PK where sometimes you would continously laugh and giggle. I really enjoyed watching this movie. And I recommend you to also watch thiss movie. I rate this 7 stars out of 5. Rally touching interesting film.

Watching "3 Idiots" was like embarking on a rollercoaster ride of emotions, laughter, and profound realizations. From the moment the film began, I was drawn into the vibrant world of Indian engineering students, where the pressures of academic excellence collide with the pursuit of genuine passion and understanding. The film's narrative is both humorous and heartwarming, making it easy to relate to the characters' struggles and aspirations.What struck me most was the film's ability to balance comedy with serious themes. The witty dialogues and laugh-out-loud moments were perfectly juxtaposed with poignant lessons about life, education, and friendship. The mantra "All is well" resonated with me long after the credits rolled, serving as a reminder to approach life's challenges with a positive mindset.Aamir Khan's portrayal of Rancho was nothing short of brilliant. His character's unconventional approach to education and life inspired not just his friends in the film but also me as a viewer. The supporting cast, including Sharman Joshi and R. Madhavan, brought depth to the story, each adding their unique flair and emotional weight. Boman Irani as the strict yet complex professor was a standout, embodying the rigid educational system that the film critiques.The cinematography was stunning, capturing the essence of college life in India, from the bustling campus to the serene moments of reflection. The music, while not groundbreaking, complemented the narrative beautifully, enhancing the emotional beats without overshadowing the story.One of the most impactful aspects of "3 Idiots" is its commentary on the education system. It challenges the notion that grades define success and emphasizes the importance of pursuing one's passion. This message is universal, transcending cultural boundaries, and I found myself reflecting on my own educational experiences and the pressures that come with them.As the film reached its climax, I was left in awe. The unexpected twists and emotional revelations hit hard, making the experience all the more memorable. "3 Idiots" is not just a film; it's a celebration of friendship, dreams, and the courage to defy societal expectations.In conclusion, "3 Idiots" is a cinematic gem that I wholeheartedly recommend. It's a film that will make you laugh, cry, and think deeply about what truly matters in life. Whether you're a student, a parent, or just someone navigating the complexities of life, this film has something valuable to offer. Don't miss it!

After watching this film, I believe that the main message it wants to convey is that passion is the driving force for learning. Our exam-oriented education system has overlooked this point, merely instilling knowledge into children without ever considering whether they have the enthusiasm to understand the knowledge. Just like a mule with its eyes covered, only grinding away, children sit in the same classroom day after day, reading and writing, without knowing what they are for. The most important thing in life is to find one's own goals and strive for them, rather than just doing whatever others tell you to do. A life without goals is pitiful. Each of us has our own path to walk. I hope that after watching this film, everyone can gain some insights and find their own life goals as soon as possible, and strive with passion. We may not become as excellent as the character Rancho, but at least we can have a life without regrets.

Review Title: 3 Idiots (2009): A Timeless Tale of Friendship, Education, and Life
Review Text: In the realm of Bollywood cinema, "3 Idiots" stands out as a timeless masterpiece that seamlessly blends humor, heart, and a profound message about the education system and the pursuit of true happiness. Released in 2009 and directed by Rajkumar Hirani, this film not only entertained audiences but also left an indelible mark on the cultural landscape.At its core, "3 Idiots" is a story of friendship, self-discovery, and the courage to challenge societal norms. The narrative follows three engineering students-Rancho (played by Aamir Khan), Farhan (played by R. Madhavan), and Raju (played by Sharman Joshi)-as they navigate the pressures of academic life at the prestigious Imperial College of Engineering. The film takes a humorous and satirical approach to address the serious issues within the education system, exposing its flaws and questioning the conventional definition of success.Aamir Khan's portrayal of Rancho is a standout performance, capturing the essence of a free-spirited and unconventional thinker. The chemistry between the three protagonists is palpable, creating moments of genuine laughter and heartfelt emotion. The supporting cast, including Boman Irani and Kareena Kapoor Khan, adds depth to the narrative with their impeccable performances.The film's cinematography and music further enhance the viewing experience. The breathtaking landscapes, vibrant campus scenes, and energetic musical numbers contribute to the overall visual and emotional appeal. The iconic song "All Izz Well" became an anthem of optimism, resonating with audiences and reinforcing the film's central message.What sets "3 Idiots" apart is its ability to balance humor with a thought-provoking exploration of societal expectations and individual aspirations. The screenplay, adapted from Chetan Bhagat's novel "Five Point Someone," successfully weaves together comedy, drama, and social commentary, making it relatable to a diverse audience.As the film progresses, it transcends its genre, evolving into a philosophical exploration of life's purpose and the pursuit of happiness. The memorable dialogue, such as "Chase excellence, success will follow," continues to inspire and resonate with viewers, reinforcing the film's enduring impact.In conclusion, "3 Idiots" is not just a film; it's a cinematic journey that encourages viewers to question the status quo, pursue their passions, and prioritize happiness over societal expectations. A perfect blend of humor, emotion, and social commentary, this Bollywood gem has rightfully earned its place as a classic that continues to captivate audiences, leaving a lasting impression for generations to come.

3 Idiots is an Indian film directed by Rajkumar Hirani which was released in 2009. The movie 3 Idiots is based on Indian educational system which pressurizes students and prioritizes high grades. The movie is about a clever student, Rancho who disagree with the educational system and persuade his 2 friends to pursue their passion. His friends are Farhan who passionate about wildlife photography but reluctantly chose to join engineering in order to appease his father, and Raju who is pursuing engineering to get his family out of extreme poverty. The first half of the film includes ragging scene, confrontations with the professor Viru, Chatur's speech and mournful meal at Raju's home.The film depicts an acceptable blend of comedy, suspense and motivation. The film is set in two main locations that are university and the hills that gives it a very beautiful atmosphere. All the actors and co-actors done a praiseworthy job. The soundtrack of the film was great with a strong Indian vibe. The background music supports the film all through the movie. Some of the scenes are really emotional.The conclusion are We're very recommend you to watch this film even though this film was made in 2009 this was a great movie it has a lot of moral value that we can learn, it has a great story, the actor was acting so good and also the quality of the picture not bad for 2009 movie. Still worth to watch this movie in 2021. This movie also has great sense of humor its suitable and very recommend for you who looking for a comedy genre film with a great story. In the end of this movie review we want to say good job for the movie director and all of the movie crew for making this movie.
