"3 Idiots" is an evergreen Bollywood movie. The Aamir Khan starrer movie is a great mixture of several genre like comedy ,drama and romance. I highly recommend this movie. The movie talks about several life experiences in humorous tones. Other great actors like Sharman Joshi aka 'Raju' , Madhavan aka 'Farhan' , Omi Vaidya as 'Silencer' , KKK as 'Piya' and Boman Irani as 'Virus' made this movie a huge success.The movie revolves around 3 BFF's in an engineering college having different perspectives and backgrounds yet share a deep bond with each other. The movie comes with a plethora of surprises and great plots. This movie depicts life of engineering students which I believe will be an inspiration for all the generations to come. The movie shows several sensitive topics like suicide, ragging and poverty yet produces a very strong and clear message for the audience. The movie gives several life lessons and is a treat to one's eye. The songs in the movie and depth of the characters as well as the story bring a spectacular effect in the movie which makes it worth to watch, even several times.All in all the movie is a brilliant collaboration of great themes, innovative ideas and strong acting making it a blockbuster and registering it's name in the golden book of history.

This is a three hour long film, although long, but not "smelly", from beginning to end without a redundant plot, the beginning and the end of the film before and after the echo, let the film structure more compact. On average every 15 minutes to shake a burden to increase the comedy, every 25 minutes to a dance to refresh, the director of this pot "Indian chaos stew" do very much in line with the audience's taste.
3 idiots proves that good movies don't have to be bitter, and that it's enough to keep audiences entertained. "All is well", "you are so afraid of tomorrow, how can you live tomorrow"... A word of wisdom goes straight to the heart. We should live in the present, regardless of the gains and losses, dare to make choices to their own life, finally can not leave regrets.

Watching "3 Idiots" was an experience that transcended mere entertainment; it was a journey through the highs and lows of student life, wrapped in humor and poignant lessons. As someone who has navigated the pressures of academia, I found the film's exploration of the Indian education system both relatable and eye-opening. The narrative cleverly juxtaposes the relentless pursuit of grades with the essence of true learning, a theme that resonates universally.The characters, brought to life by a stellar cast, felt like old friends. Aamir Khan's portrayal of Rancho was both charming and inspiring, embodying the spirit of curiosity and rebellion against the status quo. His interactions with his friends, played by R. Madhavan and Sharman Joshi, were filled with genuine camaraderie and laughter, making their journey all the more engaging. Boman Irani as the strict yet complex professor added depth to the story, showcasing the often rigid structures within educational institutions.What struck me most was the film's ability to balance humor with serious undertones. The comedic moments, from the bathroom dance sequences to the outrageous pranks, were not just for laughs; they served to highlight the absurdity of the pressures placed on students. I found myself laughing out loud, yet also reflecting on my own experiences and the societal expectations that often overshadow the joy of learning.The cinematography was vibrant, capturing the essence of college life in India, while the soundtrack, though initially unremarkable, grew on me as it perfectly complemented the film's emotional beats. The message of "Aal Izz Well" became a personal mantra, reminding me that while challenges may arise, maintaining a positive outlook can make all the difference.As the credits rolled, I felt a sense of fulfillment. "3 Idiots" is more than just a film; it's a celebration of friendship, dreams, and the pursuit of knowledge. It left me motivated to chase my passions and reminded me that success is not merely about grades, but about understanding and applying what we learn. I wholeheartedly recommend this film to anyone, regardless of their background, as it offers valuable insights wrapped in a delightful package of humor and heart.

Review Title: 3 Idiots - Story Of Three College Buddies
Review Text: Starring: : Aamir Khan, Kareena Kapoor, R Madhavan, Sharman Joshi, Boman Irani. Direction: Rajkumar Hirani Music: Shantanu Moitra Production: Vidhu Vinod Chopra ProductionsThe story is about two friends Farhan (Madhavan) and Raju (Sharman) who on getting a clue on the whereabouts of their college friend, Rancho (Aamir), start a quest to find him. Rancho in his college days was an exceptional, but 3 idiotsThe movie is a joyful ride from start to finish with the pace tilting downwards only in the middle of the second half. Few good twists and the non-linear style of storytelling help the proceedings in a big way too. But Rajkumar Hirani borrows most of his jokes from the net and also does go filmy in a few scenes like the Sharman interview sequence or Aamir crying for every little thing that his friend does, or the scene where Mona Singh is having a baby and the scene after that. But scenes like the speech by Omi for teacher's day, or the first time Rancho visits Raju's house or Mona Singh's marriage, helps in bringing down the entire theater with laughter. Initially, getting to believe Madhavan or Aamir as college students might be quite tough, but as the screenplay moves, we just start enjoying the movie and their acting. Aamir Khan is spectacular as Rancho and age aside, we can't think of a better Rancho after seeing the movie. Madhavan might not be looking as a college student but he brings a new dimension to his acting repertoire - a brilliant act. Sharman shows his excellence in a role which seems to be written for him. Kareena looks cute and does her role with equal cuteness. Boman Irani is brilliant, but what's new in it? Mona Singh is good in a small part. The actor enacting the role Omi is easily the find of the season; he is exceptional in almost all his scenes and is a riot to watch. It's been quite a while since we saw the entire theater clapping, laughing aloud and whistling with you. It's a complete theater going experience. 3 Idiots is easily going to be the number one movie for quite a while at the box-office and can very well be the number one movie of the year. Hirani manages to give an excellent entertainer with a subtle message one more time.

Review Title: 3 Idiots - Story Of Three College Buddies
Review Text: Starring: : Aamir Khan, Kareena Kapoor, R Madhavan, Sharman Joshi, Boman Irani. Direction: Rajkumar Hirani Music: Shantanu Moitra Production: Vidhu Vinod Chopra ProductionsThe story is about two friends Farhan (Madhavan) and Raju (Sharman) who on getting a clue on the whereabouts of their college friend, Rancho (Aamir), start a quest to find him. Rancho in his college days was an exceptional, but 3 idiotsThe movie is a joyful ride from start to finish with the pace tilting downwards only in the middle of the second half. Few good twists and the non-linear style of storytelling help the proceedings in a big way too. But Rajkumar Hirani borrows most of his jokes from the net and also does go filmy in a few scenes like the Sharman interview sequence or Aamir crying for every little thing that his friend does, or the scene where Mona Singh is having a baby and the scene after that. But scenes like the speech by Omi for teacher's day, or the first time Rancho visits Raju's house or Mona Singh's marriage, helps in bringing down the entire theater with laughter. Initially, getting to believe Madhavan or Aamir as college students might be quite tough, but as the screenplay moves, we just start enjoying the movie and their acting. Aamir Khan is spectacular as Rancho and age aside, we can't think of a better Rancho after seeing the movie. Madhavan might not be looking as a college student but he brings a new dimension to his acting repertoire - a brilliant act. Sharman shows his excellence in a role which seems to be written for him. Kareena looks cute and does her role with equal cuteness. Boman Irani is brilliant, but what's new in it? Mona Singh is good in a small part. The actor enacting the role Omi is easily the find of the season; he is exceptional in almost all his scenes and is a riot to watch. It's been quite a while since we saw the entire theater clapping, laughing aloud and whistling with you. It's a complete theater going experience. 3 Idiots is easily going to be the number one movie for quite a while at the box-office and can very well be the number one movie of the year. Hirani manages to give an excellent entertainer with a subtle message one more time.

Review Title: After 'Jaadu ki jhappi' and 'Gandhigiri' its 'aal izz well'
Review Text: In a late scene from the film, 2 of the 3 idiots zip down their pants and pat their bottoms facing the third idiot. Rather than feeling gross at that instant, you have a lump in throat. For the zillionth time in a Hindi film, a bride runs away from the pheras on her wedding day. But the scene still doesn't come across as clichéd. For a group of all-male engineering students, to 'deliver' means to literally carry out a delivery operation on a woman in labour pain. And when the motionless newborn responds to a goodwill chant of 'aal izz well' in true-blue Manmohan Desai mode, you fail to find a fault in the filmi formula. That's the golden touch of Rajkumar Hirani.So after successfully dispensing philosophies of 'Jaadu ki jhappi' in Munnabhai MBBS and 'Gandhigiri' in Lage Raho Munnabhai, Hirani incites a new philosophy of 'Aal izz well' in 3 Idiots. And like his doctrine, all is well in his helluva film.Loosely based on Chetan Bhagat's bestseller Five Point Someone, 3 Idiots takes the plot much beyond the campus confines and the target audience much above the youth, for universal appeal. The story starts a decade after the graduation of college companions Farhan (R Madhavan) and Raju Rastogi (Sharman Joshi) who get a clue on the whereabouts of their missing third friend Rancho (Aamir Khan). As they set out on a road trip from Delhi to Shimla to Manali to Ladakh to find their friend, the narrative cuts to and fro into flashbacks as we are introduced to the three idiots in an engineering college.Rancho clearly is different from anyone else in the college with his individualistic thought-process and rebellious attitude, which invites the ire of the college principal (Boman Irani) and affection of his daughter Pia (Kareena Kapoor).Coming from Rajkumar Hirani, there was clearly a risk involved in the film's setting and characterizations for having a déjà vu effect with his first film. Boman Irani, as the principal, almost revives his disciplinarian dean characterization from Munnabhai MBBS which is more palpable with his disgust towards the rebellious protagonist, Rancho (akin to Sanjay Dutt) who is furthermore in love with his daughter (ala Gracy Singh). Nevertheless, Hirani's direction is so impeccable that without a conscious effort, the analogy never strikes your mind and the scenario never looks repetitive.In his trademark style, Hirani grabs your attention from scene one with an unconventional opening to the film. Thereafter every single scene written in the screenplay (by Hirani and Abhijat Joshi) is not just relevant but also has a clear set objective – to be funny or be deeply poignant. Which means it either makes you laugh or cry and at some superlative instances do both simultaneously (which is an achievement). The writers have kept absolutely no room for any intermediate option.

Review Title: A Heartfelt Journey of Friendship, Dreams, and Education Director: Rajkumar Hirani
Review Text:
3 Idiots, directed by Rajkumar Hirani, is a brilliant blend of humor, drama, and social commentary that explores the pressures of the Indian education system through the lens of friendship and self-discovery. With its witty screenplay, memorable performances, and emotional depth, the film remains a beloved classic in Indian cinema.Plot Summary
The story revolves around three friends-Rancho (Aamir Khan), Farhan (R. Madhavan), and Raju (Sharman Joshi)-who meet at the prestigious Imperial College of Engineering. Rancho, a free-spirited and innovative thinker, profoundly impacts the lives of his friends and challenges the conventional education system.The narrative oscillates between the past, where the trio navigates the trials of college life, and the present, where Farhan and Raju embark on a journey to find Rancho, who has mysteriously disappeared after graduation. Along the way, they reflect on the lessons learned and the impact Rancho had on their lives.Performances
Aamir Khan delivers an outstanding performance as Rancho, effortlessly embodying the character's curiosity, charisma, and rebellious spirit. His portrayal adds depth and sincerity to the film's message about the importance of following one's passion.R. Madhavan as Farhan and Sharman Joshi as Raju are equally impressive, providing a balanced mix of humor and emotion. Their on-screen camaraderie feels genuine, making the bond between the three friends the heart of the film.Kareena Kapoor Khan, as Pia, brings charm and warmth to her role as Rancho's love interest, while Boman Irani excels as the strict and eccentric college dean, Viru Sahastrabuddhe (Virus). Omi Vaidya's portrayal of Chatur Ramalingam, the overly competitive student, is both hilarious and memorable, particularly during his infamous speech scene.Direction and Screenplay
Rajkumar Hirani's direction is exemplary, skillfully balancing comedy, drama, and social critique. The screenplay, co-written by Hirani and Abhijat Joshi, is sharp, engaging, and filled with witty dialogues that resonate with audiences of all ages.The film's pacing is well-managed, ensuring that the narrative remains captivating throughout its runtime. The clever integration of flashbacks and the present-day storyline keeps the audience invested in the characters' journeys.Themes and Execution
3 Idiots tackles several critical themes, including the flaws in the education system, the pressure of parental expectations, and the pursuit of one's dreams. The film encourages viewers to question societal norms and emphasizes the value of creativity, innovation, and self-belief.One of the film's most poignant messages is encapsulated in Rancho's mantra, "All is well," which serves as a reminder to stay positive and resilient in the face of challenges. The film's humor and emotional moments are skillfully interwoven, making its messages more impactful.Music and Cinematography
The soundtrack, composed by Shantanu Moitra, is a delightful addition to the film, with songs like "Aal Izz Well," "Zoobi Doobi," and "Give Me Some Sunshine" becoming instant hits. The music enhances the narrative's emotional and comedic elements, making the viewing experience more enjoyable.C. K. Muraleedharan's cinematography captures the vibrancy of college life and the scenic beauty of various locations. The film's visual storytelling is complemented by its well-designed sets and engaging montages, particularly during the flashback sequences.Conclusion
3 Idiots is more than just a comedy; it is a thought-provoking and heartwarming tale that resonates with anyone who has faced the pressures of academic life or questioned their path. With its stellar performances, insightful narrative, and unforgettable moments, the film continues to inspire and entertain audiences worldwide.For those seeking a film that combines humor with a meaningful message about education and self-discovery, 3 Idiots is a must-watch. It stands as a testament to the power of friendship, the importance of pursuing one's passion, and the enduring impact of questioning the status quo.

Pants down and Palms up!...A well directed cocktail by the 'Munnabhai' series famed director RajKumar Hirani,consists of good Humour n a social message,feel-good movie with a perfect entertainment quotient but somehow this very film failed to be called as a 'Masterpiece'...!!! ... *DIRECTION: We all know that Mr. Hirani always brings something new to his film...& this time he did not disappoint us too...Though the film is based on the fiction "Five Point Someone" of Chetan Bhagat,the film has its selfness due to it's awesome direction...!!! 8/10 ... * STORY: May the structure of the story remains unchanged but majority of the subplots n contexts were rewritten as the prime storyline which differs it from the original book-version of it n few of those changes seemed unnecessary n impractical to make it as a Film version with some Desi flavor of 'Five Point Someone'...! 8/10 ... * SCREENPLAY & Editing: Screenplay with new reformed storyline consists of some delectable 'idiotgiri',juvenile humors n tear-shedding moments but problem is that the core message got hammered out sometimes on the basis of Reality vs. 'Reel'ity...! Editing was good...! 7/10 ... * ACTING: Its the strongest part of the film!...Its a curious n eventful journey about 3 Friends who got admidmssion to a reputed Engineering College n all the threes were shared equal spaces on screen...So, its difficult to choose the main Protagonist...!!! Mr. Perfectionist Aamir Khan did another memorable n well apprising performance as Rancho... Sharman Joshi sparked as Raju n i think he spent most time on screen with his spontaneous appearance... R.Madhavan as Farhan did an excellent job as well as a narrator n also an actor... Kareena Kapoor was always a pleasure... Omi Vaidya did an amazing debut to the B-Town portraying the character of Chatur (Silencer)... Others were perfect at their roles but the Antagonist Boman Irani was the best among all....He Viru Sahastrabuddhe a.k.a. 'Virus' stood out in every criteria of shades of acting n impressed the audience most...IMO, the character of Javed Jaffrey went impractial sometimes...! 10/10 ... * MUSIC: This film shines with it's melodious n lovely Music composed by Shantanu Mukherjee... 'Jaane Nahin Denge Tujhe', 'Behti Hawa Sa Tha Woh' n 'Zoobi Doobi' are my personal favorites... 10/10 ... In a country where students are driven to suicide by their impossible curriculum, 3 Idiots provides hope. Maybe cinema can't save lives. But cinema, sure as hell, can make you feel life is worth living...& this is the significance of the film...But, the message "Success nahi,Excellence k piche bhago" did not seem realistic at all n unable to accommodate with our modern Education system as well as our daily life...! .... Anyway, Kudos to the whole team of the film for giving us a gripping,entertaining,humorous Comedy film with some good messages...A movie to be cherished for refreshing everyone's mind... Good one n treat to watch it! ..... Rating: 8/10 Must watch!

Review Title: One of the best Bollywood movies
Review Text: Watched this movie over a dozen times and it never disappoints to entertain and lighten the environment. It's humorous and fresh take on seemingly regular but deeply held Indian societal notions is quite incredible throughout the movie. The screenplay, direction are top notch, sustaining the good work throughout the movie including delivering a punch to topics which other movies rather avoids. The actor and entire unit seems equally enjoyed their part and rubs that to the viewers without leaving a feeling of dragging ever, including making the songs relevant to the plot. The movie is not only highly watchable but the best from the Bollywood stable.

Review Title: "Bachcha Kabil Bano, Kabil.. Kamyabi Toh Saali Jhak Maarke Peeche Bhagegi."
Review Text:
Kudos to director Rajkumar Hirani to doing something more than justice to Chetan Bhagat's novel five point someone. The story revolves around 3 friends namely Farhan (R. Madhavan), Raju (Sharman Joshi) and Rancho (Aamir Khan) and their journey of 4 years through an engineering college.
The film begins with Farhan and Raju going on a searching spree for Rancho after 10 years of their college life has ended. Over the whole of journey of road they keep on lamenting the memories of their elite college life. It takes the first trust with the mandatory ragging session which enunciates who the leader of the gang is going to be. At this point enters the ever charming Aamir Khan as Rancchodas. Rancho not only led his friends through all the mazes of peer pressure, illogical educational system but also taught them some excruciating exciting life mantras.
Through the journey of college life, the three musketeers came in a confrontation with the authority as represented through the dominating figure of Viru Sahastrabuddhe who's only principle to life is, to win the race of life you need to be fast or else "you'll be like the broken egg of a cuckoo bird." Doesn't he know that competition is effete, model students like Chatur (Omi) end up as duhs in real life and non-conformists like Rancho who care tuppence about being on top, could end up as eventual winners. More importantly, they could be high not only in IQ (intelligence quotient) but in EQ (emotional quotient) too, never losing their humaneness and social networking skills.
In the process of travelling through the journey of college life, Rancho falls in love with Pia (Kareena Kapoor) who happens to be Viru Sahastrabuddhe's daughter and they inspite of all the odds in the path as destiny has carved out, Ranchho and Pia do end up together after a long wait of 10 years.
The second half of the movie holds special mention due to its sudden turn of events as of Raju's suicide attempt to the child birth sequence. But it doesn't take long for the film to jump back on track.
The best part of the film is that, this film is a factory of life lessons yet Hirani for not even once loses the humor essence of the movie. Certain sequences might even have you rolling on the floor, like the ragging sequence, Omi's "chamatkar" speech, the mournful meal with Raju's mom, attending Pia's sister's wedding, Ranchos sundry demo of how Pia has chosen the wrong guy for himself.
3 idiots is a complete package watch from comedy to seriousness to life lessons to carry with yourself throughout. Along with the incredible acting of all the actors, Shantanu Moitra's music score gave the movie a whole new level of life.
Having being released in the year 2009, 3 idiots still now holds a good rank in our hearts when reminded of a Sunday Afternoon Movie Marathon with family!

Through the journey of college life, the three musketeers came in a confrontation with the authority as represented through the dominating figure of Viru Sahastrabuddhe who's only principle to life is, to win the race of life you need to be fast or else "you'll be like the broken egg of a cuckoo bird." Doesn't he know that competition is effete, model students like Chatur (Omi) end up as duhs in real life and non-conformists like Rancho who care tuppence about being on top, could end up as eventual winners. More importantly, they could be high not only in IQ (intelligence quotient) but in EQ (emotional quotient) too, never losing their humaneness and social networking skills.

Review Title: tells us a life learning message ,without giving depression
Review Text: I found this movie relate able to my academic life because I'm an engineering student and this movie also revolves around different scenarios related to three engineering students. It wonderfully tells us some serious messages in a comedy genre, otherwise this movie could become a piece of depression for me. The concept in the movie that I found positive was that it conveys a message of not encouraging cramming in studies. We should be able to understand and implement concept. Another thing that I considered good in this movie is that you should always chase your subject of interest otherwise you might get a good lifestyle and job, but you will surely not be going to enjoy your profession. An issue has also been raised through this movie, that is family pressure on a student along with academic pressure. A student might be able to handle the academic pressure but when it comes to family pressure, he starts giving more focus on good grades instead of good learning. It depicts a teacher student relation but somehow in a negative way. Teacher plays the most significant role in any student's carrier. Due to teacher's behavior, a student can simply hate or love a subject. It also tells us about the awful education system, that has become a trend in our society. But the characters in this movie gives little importance to rules and regulation of university, which is a negative thing for me, because it is not possible for a student to show a careless behavior towards discipline related issues. As student should not only focus on how to become a good engineer, but also focuses to become a good human being for the society.

Review Title: Most Entertaining Bollywood Movie till Date
Review Text: I am enthralled by the movie and would watch it again.I was taken back in time to my own college days and really for a moment I thought as though I was in involved in the story. The senseless competitiveness unmindful of the dreams of children and their ambitions was the central theme of the story. The song " Give me some sunshine " was a real tear jerker. I also wish I could go back in time and get a chance to do something different.Acting by the leads were awesome. Boman Irani as the professor VIRUS was outstanding and I think one of the best too. Not to mention Silencer, (OMI) who plays the rattu popat also was very good indeed. Chetan Bhagat from whose novel (3 point someone) this movie based deserves credit, but unfortunately his name is not included. I look forward to watch such great Bollywood movies in the future. This movie has left such an impression on me, that I find it hard to watch any other movie lately. Kudos to the director for a fine movie

Cast:Aamir Khan(Rancho) Madhvan(Farhan Qureshi) Sharman Joshi(Raju Rastogi) Omi Vaidya(Chatur) Boman Irani(Virus) Kareena Kapoor(Pia)3 idiots is one of the finest movies Bollywood has ever produced.It talks about the pressures,the fun and the anxiety regarding the future of engineering students.It had been an all time blockbuster and was one of the greatest hits of 2009.The movie begins as Farhan and get to know about their old friend Rancho who vanished after college graduation ceremony.The source of information was Chatur who always tried to be superior than other and who is eager to verify that he has become bigger success than Rancho.Farhan,Raju and Chatur all three starts their journey to find Rancho.The movie flashes back into their college days.Farhan wanted to become a photographer but engineering is where the money is at in India.He didn't want to disappoint his parents.Raju was from a poor family.He can't afford having poor grades.Chatur was a person highly obsessed with grades and followed the motto:"There are two ways to to do well in exams,1.Study 2.Dont let anyone to study".Rancho didn't worry about getting good grades.He was passionate about engineering.Pia is trapped between choosing to keep family tradition by obeying her father for marriage or following her heart.Viru Shahastra Buddhe was the director of the college.He never liked Rancho in the college days but eventually earned Virus' coveted pen which he had received for being an exemplary student.On the way of searching Rancho,Farhan and Raju came to know about Pia's marriage.They decide to go to venue and tell Pia about the information about Rancho.Pia decides to quit the marriage ceremony and look for Rancho.They came to know that Rancho was studying for someone else in the college.What Rancho had learned helped him establish his own line of work as an inventor and teacher later on anyway even if his achievement in the premiere engineering school had been given up to someone else. The reason behind Rancho's disappearance — which is explained halfway through the movie.3 idiots is a smart comedy.Jokes are well written.There are few melodramatic moments but they are not overdone.The movie is entertaining throughout which keeps the viewers' attention alive.One can say that 3 idiots is one of the best movies of Bollywood.

3 Idiots were directed by Rajkumar Hirani and scripted by Abhijat Joshi.
The story of the film is about India's rude-crude education system, which does not encourage students to choose their destination by their wish, rather than it forces us to follow the regular rules of the system like the rat race.In this film, we see a lot of characters. In between them Farhan (R Madhavan), Raju (Sharman Joshi) & Rancho(Aamir Khan) are the main leading characters. They got admitted to the city's elite college, where they meet their principal Viru Sahastrabuddhe (Boman Irani). Who was very strict with his rules. For that everyone in the college was afraid of him. But in behind every one call him VIRUS.Then comes rancho with a joyful and limitless, free soul, Who always gives challenges to the difficulties of life, takes it very lightly.
Raju and Farhan are different. They are afraid of parents, teachers, and also exams. Both of them have their different family problems.
In this movie, we see the coming of Rancho in their life changes the viewing angle of them and also their way of thinking. Here we also can see an interesting character Chatur (Omi), Who always follows the race and want to become the winner.
Though it is a very thoughtful and enthusing film, presence of Priya(
Kareena Kapoor) makes it quite romantic and emotional. And the small argues with VIRUS entertaining the audience, especially the Chamatkar/Balatkar speech.
Music & Choreography are really awesome. By watching this movie I personally enjoyed it a lot, and also inspired too. I must recommend you to see this movie at least once in your life.

3 idiots is not about idiots in literal sense but about talented guys who needs a motivating push that has been missing in our highly reputed institutions. Narrated in a sarcastic tone, it says about Rancho, a passionate but mysterious engineering student who teaches how to learn, how to enjoy life, how to be happy despite the tensions of the competitive world. He is against the flow of the world and societal views of success. The society views him as idiot and his two roommates too who were influenced by him. Through many adventures of ups and downs, through many happy moments in the engineering college they finally reunites in a hilly region detailed in the entire runtime. The film is depicted in flashbacks by one of his roommates Farhan reminiscing his experience with Rancho (Amir khan).The comic theme and education are both strong and well relates to students. No students will hate 3 idiots for these reasons. Romance and suspense are added treat well placed by Mr Hirani too. It studies our indian society surpassing religion and traditions paying attention to root reasons like eduation system or the families involved. Rancho's example could be summed into one line - 'Listen to the heart and be free'. The presentation of the movie is nice too. There's also a twist about who Rancho really is.Summing up, the film teaches, gives messages, entertains us with some of the real life characters that we all have experienced in some parts of our lives. The film has deprecated realism significantly to suit the greater audience making it a commercial success, no doubt about it. Thus R. Hirani has given us a mass appealing entertaining movie for students, and for adults who like to think themselves as lifelong students. Rating wise it's an 8/10 for its strong entertainment value, stronger than its messages.

Cast: Amir Khan, Madhaan, Sharman Joshi, Karena Kapoor Khan, Omi Vaidya and Boman Irani
Director: Rajkumar Hirani
Synopsis: One of my favorite movie, which is funny, emotional and educational. A story of three funny best friends of an engineering college in India, whose family background was totally different from each other. Chatur "Silencer" Ramalingam give a call to his college mates Raju Rastogi and Farhan Qureshi for a reunion with Rancho after ten years of their graduation. Chatur finds a rarer picture of Rancho who was not in connection with them since their graduation day did not have any information about him. Now, Raju, Farhan, and Chatur started their journey to meet Rancho in Shimla, India. In a journey to meet Rancho, Farhan is recalling all of the memories from their first - last day of college. Their college experience was challenging in every step but also Rancho challenges his college dean Viru Sahastrabudhhe "Virus" every day. Review: First time I have watched this movie, I think it was confusing, worthless and timewasting until I watch the movie at home with my parents. I had watched the first day, first show with my best friends and we critic whole movie worthless. We used to like action, dramatic and documentaries movies until we have watched this movie for the second time. Since then this is one of my favorite movies forever. One of the take of way from this movie was Rancho dialogue " All is well" meaning when ever you have to deal with difficulties movement in your life, forget everything, place right hand in left chest and say "All is well" to decrease your heartbeat. One of my favorite sense from the movie is when that Chatur gives the speech in teacher day in front of the minister, principle, dean of college including teacher and students. Virus advised Farhan and Raju to be aware of Rancho and start their new life along with Chatur. Farhan ignored Virus advice but Raju thinks it was turning point for him so he started a friendship with Chatur. Now, Farhan and Rancho want to give Chatur hard time and lesson to Raju by changing scripts. Since Chatur was born in Uganda and studying in English, he Hindi was very weak. So, Farhan and Rancho take this opportunity to changed teacher day scripts using slang words in the middle of a paragraph. Now, when Chatur started speech, the whole auditorium laughs because scripts were not only funny, however, every word gives negative sense to insulting teachers and minster.The movie contains the good quality of sounds, mixing, color contraction and animation. They have used animation of human sperms to emphasize Farhan birth and Raju critical condition in hospital. They have used silence as a sound effect in Raju sense where he jumped from the terrace committed suicide after meeting with Virus. The contrast sensitivity of Raju, Rancho, and Farhan drinking alcohol under moonlight looks pure and natural. Finally, the title of the movie intended to be funny, but also, I viewed the movie as educational, dramatic and emotional. Every character of movie contributes a lot to be one of the best films including the director himself play a varied role including editing and writing. This is one of best movie for college students, parents, and teachers. This movie was rated 8.3 out of 10 which is pretty impressive and I think this movie deserve it.

3 Idiots looks at our educational system which pressurizes the students and does not allow to chose his destination by his own wish, in a satirical way and brings out some of the flaws in it... On parallel there is a love angle between Rancho and Pia (Kareena)... Many of the situations/characters have resemblance or inspiration to "Five Point Someone", blockbuster novel penned by Chetan Bhagat...Both humour and emotional quotients are amalgamated in the right proportion, full credits to Raju for that... Film offers lots of quality rib-tickling moments throughout.. The intermission point offers a twist that is totally out of the blue and keeps you hooked to the movie ! Just when you think this feel-good comic caper is leading to its culmination, there is another surprising element at the climax which is brilliantly weaved out by Hirani...Technically, it rocks with astounding and visually arresting cinematography, the aerial shots of Shimla and Ladakh are a pleasant treat for our eyes... Editing was good... Song visualizations were cool... Music was good and well placed with the flow of the narration...Coming to performances, all of them comes out with convincing portrayals, but it is Aamir Khan, the real showman of Bollywood, steals the shows with yet another stellar portrayal and will go down as one of his best performances. His looks and mannerisms as a college student was genuine and consummate.. Madhavan and Sharman adds up with commendable performances... Kareena does her part well... Among the supporting, Boman Irani as the eccentric head of the institution was too good.

Watching "3 Idiots" was an experience that transcended mere entertainment; it was a journey through the complexities of education, friendship, and the pursuit of passion. From the moment the film began, I was drawn into the vibrant world of Indian engineering students, where humor and poignant moments seamlessly intertwined.The film's narrative, centered around the lives of three friends, resonated deeply with me, especially as someone who has navigated the pressures of academic life. The character of Rancho, played brilliantly by Aamir Khan, embodies a refreshing perspective on education that challenges the conventional norms. His mantra, "All is well," became a comforting reminder that while life may throw challenges our way, maintaining a positive outlook is crucial.What struck me most was how the film tackled serious themes with a lighthearted touch. The comedic elements, particularly the outrageous bathroom dance sequence, had me laughing out loud, while the more serious moments made me reflect on my own experiences and the societal pressures surrounding academic success. The performances were outstanding; each actor brought depth to their roles, making their struggles and triumphs feel incredibly relatable.The cinematography was stunning, capturing the essence of college life in India, and the music, though initially seeming mediocre, perfectly complemented the film's emotional highs and lows. Raju Hirani's direction and screenplay were masterful, crafting a narrative that kept me engaged for nearly three hours without feeling the weight of time.As I left the theater, I felt inspired to chase my own dreams rather than simply conforming to societal expectations. "3 Idiots" is not just a film; it's a movement that encourages viewers to value knowledge over rote learning and to embrace their individuality. It's a film I would recommend to anyone, regardless of their background, as it holds universal truths about life and education. I walked away not just entertained, but also with a renewed sense of purpose and a smile on my face.

3 idiots is a good example for students who believe on ratta .
The film begins with entry of threesome idiots in the city's elite engineering college. It takes the first trust with the mandatory ragging session which enunciate who the leader of the gang is going to be: new entrant Baba Rancchoddas, as his friends fondly call him. Rancho not only leads his friends through the maze of India's competitive, high-pressure, rote-heavy, illogical and almost cruel education system, he tutors them on several life mantras too. Like, running after excellence, not success; questioning not blindly accepting givens; inventing and experimenting in lieu of copying and
cramming; and essentially following your heart's calling if you truly want to make a difference.
So, you have the threesome embroiled, time and again, in a confrontation with authority, as represented through the domineering figure of Viru Sahastrabuddhe (Boman Irani), the unsmiling Principal who venerates the cuckoo because the bird's life begins with murder. Kill the competition, because there is only one place at the top, believes the Princi. Poor, mistaken Princi! Doesn't he know that competition is effete, model students like Chatur (Omi) end up as duhs in real life and non-
conformists (Rancho and Rocket Singh Inc), who care tuppence about being on top, could end up as eventual winners. More importantly, they could be high not only in IQ (intelligence quotient) but in EQ (emotional quotient) too, never losing their humaneness and social networking skills.
The high point of the film is the fact that director Rajkumar Hirani says so much, and more, without losing his sense of humour and the sheer lightness of being. The film is a laugh riot, despite being high on fundas. Certain sequences almost have you rolling in the aisle, like the ragging sequence, Omi's chamatkar/balatkar speech, the threesome's wedding crasher sequence, their mournful meal with Raju's mournful mum and Rancho's sundry demos to prove how Kareena has chosen the wrong guy for herself. Add to this, the strong emotional core of the film that makes gentle tugs, now and then, at your guts, and you have an almost perfect score. Hirani carries forward his simplistic 'humanism alone works' philosophy of the Lage Raho Munnabhai series in 3 Idiots too, making it a warm and vivacious signature tune to 2009. The second half of the film does falter in parts, specially
the child birth sequence, but it doesn't take long for the film to jump back on track.
Amongst the performances, Aamir Khan is stupendous as the rule-breaker Rancho. But the rest of the cast doesn't remain in the shadows. Both Sharman and Madhavan manage to carve their independent characters as lovable rebels too. Even Kareena shines out, despite the minuscule
length of her role. A special mention for Boman Irani who is impeccable as 'Virus', the vile Principal and newcomer Omi who perfectly slips into the stereotype of the best, albeit bakwas student. Shantanu Moitra's music score, which may have sounded pheeka in the audio version, comes alive on screen with lyricist Swanand Kirkire giving India its clarion call for 2010: Aal Izz Well. Rush for it!!.

Review Title: As an Chinese, I must say we love this movie very much!
Review Text: As a Chinese, I must say we love this movie very much! I was moved deeply! For many of us growing up in the shadow of the Gaokao (National College Entrance Exam), watching 3 Idiots is not just an entertainment experience; it feels like looking into a mirror that reflects our own struggles, fears, and suppressed dreams.The film is set in India's premier engineering college, but the atmosphere the cutthroat competition, the obsession with rankings, and the immense pressure from parents is indistinguishably similar to the Chinese educational landscape. In our culture, the "success" of a child is often viewed as the ultimate dividend of a family's investment. When the principal, Virus, tells his students that life is a race and they must "run or get trampled," it echoes the harsh reality many of us have faced since kindergarten. This shared cultural weight is why Rancho's defiance feels so heroic to us.The most impactful takeaway for me was Rancho's mantra: "Pursue excellence, and success will follow." In a society that often prioritizes pragmatism and "safe" career paths (like becoming a doctor or an engineer), we are taught to chase success defined by high salaries and social status from day one. However, the film brilliantly illustrates that when we turn ourselves into "machines" optimized for exams, we lose our humanity and curiosity. The tragedy of Joy Lobo and the transformation of Farhan and Raju serve as poignant reminders that a life lived according to someone else's script is a life wasted.What makes 3 Idiots truly exceptional is its ability to balance slapstick humor with profound social critique. One moment you are laughing at the "Chatur Speech" incident, and the next, your heart aches during the "Give Me Some Sunshine" sequence. It captures the bittersweet essence of friendship the brothers who hold your hand when you are about to jump and pull you back into the light.For a Chinese student, "All is Well" is more than just a catchy phrase; it is a psychological shield against the crushing expectations of the world. This movie taught me that education should be about "learning to learn" rather than "learning to earn." It challenged me to ask myself: Am I a machine, or am I a human being with a soul?3 Idiots is a cinematic masterpiece that transcends national borders. It is a love letter to the rebels, a wake up call to the system, and a warm embrace for every student who has ever felt like they weren't enough. It tells us that while the system may be rigid, our spirits don't have to be.

Review Title: "3 Idiots," released in 2009, is a highly acclaimed Indian Hindi-language coming-of-age comedy-drama film directed by Rajkumar Hirani.
Review Text: The story revolves around the friendship of three engineering students, Rancho (Aamir Khan), Farhan Qureshi (R. Madhavan), and Raju Rastogi (Sharman Joshi), at the prestigious Imperial College of Engineering (ICE). Rancho, with his unconventional and innovative approach to learning, clashes with the college's strict and success-driven director, Dr. Viru Sahastrabuddhe (Boman Irani), also known as "Virus."Farhan is pursuing engineering to fulfill his father's wishes despite his passion for wildlife photography, while Raju is studying to lift his family out of poverty. Rancho's philosophy of pursuing excellence rather than just grades influences his friends to think differently and follow their passions.The film unfolds through two parallel timelines: the present day, where Farhan and Raju, along with their former college rival Chatur Ramalingam (Omi Vaidya), embark on a search for their long-lost friend Rancho, and flashbacks to their college days, showcasing their experiences, friendships, and conflicts.During their college years, Rancho also develops a romantic interest in Pia (Kareena Kapoor Khan), Virus's daughter, and often challenges the rigid academic system. The film humorously and poignantly addresses the pressures of the Indian education system, the importance of following one's dreams, and the true meaning of success.

Review Title: One of the best Bollywood movie I saw
Review Text: "3 Idiots," directed by Rajkumar Hirani, is a beloved Bollywood film that combines comedy, drama, and social commentary. The film revolves around three friends - Rancho (played by Aamir Khan), Farhan (played by R. Madhavan), and Raju (played by Sharman Joshi) - who meet at an engineering college and form a deep bond. Rancho is a free-spirited and unconventional student who challenges the rigid and stressful education system. The story follows their journey through college, their struggles, successes, and the impact Rancho's philosophy has on their lives. Themes of friendship, pursuit of passion over convention, and the flaws in the educational system are central to the narrative. Aamir Khan shines as Rancho, bringing charisma and depth to his character. His portrayal of a student who questions norms and encourages his friends to follow their dreams resonates strongly with audiences. R. Madhavan and Sharman Joshi deliver heartfelt performances as Farhan and Raju, respectively, adding emotional weight to the story. The chemistry between the three leads is one of the film's strengths, making their friendship believable and endearing."3 Idiots" is a must-watch for its entertainment value as well as its profound messages about education and life. It's a film that leaves a lasting impression and continues to be cherished by audiences worldwide.

When I imagine about an Indian movie, there always is a unrealistic concepts, singing and dancing and movies are too long. But this movie totally are beyond my expectations and also it has some common things with the current Japanese society.
It is no spoiler but this movie deals with the current problems in Indian society where one's academic career decides his/her life. And there are some scenes that I have experienced in my daily lives in Japan.
For example, there is a scene a the father of one of the 3 idiots denies his dream to be a photographer because the father gave everything to send him into a good college. It is of course that academic records matter but it should not be "EVERYTHING" in one's life. 
Japanese society still tends to put the most great value on academic careers and our university exams stress us a lot. Maybe all the students have to watch it to reflect on what they have done and will do (but only after the exams are over!).

Review Title: An excellent comedy that deals with education issues
Review Text: Farhan and Raju become students in a prestigious engineering university. They feel pressure from their families and think about nothing but living up their expectations. And they meet Rancho. Unlike them, Rancho is outstanding. He always defies authority, questions convention, and invents his own way. He encourages Farhan and Raju to live their own life. The three become best friends.I like every character in the film. Rancho, played by Aamir Khan, has piercing eyes that show his genius. But he is not arrogant and always loyal to his friends. Farhan and Raju each have their weakness, and later we see their strong development. The antagonists Virus and Chatur are not really antagonized. They are funny and I like them too.The screenplay is sophisticated and every scene is cheerful. Plot devices are precisely arranged. Some objects, lines, and events reappear later to show a certain meaning. The current timeline is set in ten years later when Farhan, Raju, and Chatur travel looing for missing Rancho. This road trip part is very exhilarating in the beautiful rural landscape.The dance scene, which is a fixture for a Bollywood film, is not of women wearing a sari in a palace. Instead, it is of college boys in the bathroom wielding colorful buckets under the shower. The 'Aal Izz Well' song is such an uplifting earworm.As for the theme, it deals with issues in the education system. I don't know about the Indian situation, but it's not uncommon anywhere that education is so focused on competition that students lose their true interest and curiosity. You will see a lot of Rancho's good quotes about the issue, like "Pursue excellence, and success will follow".

Review Title: Very informative movie for a Student
Review Text:
The main purpose of this movie is students should get independence from parents when they choose their careers. Otherwise, they will suffer in college and as well as in market. When student student learn in which he / she has interest then he/ she will surely make his / her names.
Basically, this movie reflects our Asian education system. It is a reality that if student crams in school, college or university, he/she will get high grades and positions in his/her class, it is not guaranteed that he/she also get success in market as well. A student that always wants to clear his/her concepts then it is not sure that he will get high grades but it is guaranteed that he/she will be succeed in future when they will enter in market.
The role of teachers is also very important for the building the future of their students. Teachers should cooperate with student every time. In this movie, there is a harsh scene when principal of the college does not listen to his student and as a result student suicide.

This movie cannot be compared to any other movie that has or will ever be made. The comedy is original and highly laughable, but only considering it a comedy movie is a huge mistake. Even though it differs from the book 'Five Point Someone', it doesn't matter because most people don't know about the book; however, credit should be given to Chetan Bhagat). The university life experience presented here relatable to everyone, especially to students in India and Pakistan. Everytime we watch it, we find something new which we had missed before. It's a masterpiece. It will never lose its value. When you watch it during your school days, you think what are these guys doing and why are they so worried about academics and jobs. In college, you try to understand some of these things; but when you reach your university and then watch it, the dots connect amazingly backwards. It all makes sense. I don't know about what happens to a person when they watch it in their later life -- I'll try it!I consider it more just a movie; and a movie is rated on things like acting, plot, character development; but this is something which can't be quantitavely or qualitatively be measured with these metrics.

Review Title: Like a cross between Animal House (without the extreme crudeness) and Dead Poet Society
Review Text: I don't remember what path led us to this film except that when we saw Steven Spielberg quoted as loving it we added it to our list.Enthusiasm waned when we saw the description which sounded like Animal House. A 3 hour long Animal House.But then we saw it was the highest grossing Indian movie ever when it came out. Courage returned.Then we saw the movie poster. And we really wondered what we were letting ourselves in for. Coming across Big in Bollywood, a documentary following the American-born Indian cast member, bolstered Tom and Rose's courage. I hadn't seen it but went along for the 3 Idiots ride.It was something like a cross between Animal House (without the extreme crudeness) and Dead Poet Society. With some song and dance numbers thrown in because it is Bollywood, so of course. And it was surprisingly charming a lot of the time. Uneven but we weren't sorry we watched it. We didn't expect it to tackle very serious themes (that's the Dead Poet Society part) but it was done quite sensitively.Overall enjoyable as long as you are willing to go along for the ride. And the dance number with the umbrellas is adorable.

Review Title: Just Another Comedy, but Not Your Usual Comedy
Review Text: When people say that it takes a genius to make a great comedian, they really are telling the truth, because the people behind this movie are geniuses. Not only in comedy but also in human nature and emotions. This movie shows us that when you want to gain knowledge, it's not all about technical knowledge like in the books and instructions, but also about the knowledge in our hearts, the knowledge about our nature and others, and how we humanize ourselves and the people around us. This is where comedy gives us some wisdom that serious and formal institutions can't give. This movie tells us that if you want to become an intellectual and wise person, you do not always have to be serious and rigid, because we're not machines; we're people. We're humans who have hearts, and sometimes we also need to open our hearts, not just our brains.Overall, this is a good Bollywood movie, if not the best one out there, and because this is the first Indian movie that I have ever watched from beginning to end with no interruptions, I can't really give an exact comparison to other movies of this kind. However, I still have a few critiques about this movie:1. The camerawork is good, but there are still some moments where the camera is too zoomed in and feels way too exaggerated. I know that this might be commonplace in Bollywood movies, but it is still not to my taste.2. The music is good, but there are times where the music kicks in during moments that might be better off without it. I feel like the music ruins a few certain moments in this movie and, again, makes them feel a bit exaggerated.3. The pacing is a bit long-winded. I asked one of my friends and looked through several Bollywood titles, and some of them are indeed pretty long, so again, it might just be a characteristic of Bollywood movies that does not suit my taste because sometimes the pacing of a film can be destroyed by a long duration.

The second half of the film does falter in parts, specially the child birth sequence, but it doesn't take long for the film to jump back on track.

He is not jealous of anyone. He doesn't hold a grudge on anyone. He doesn't intrigue anyone. He's simple and straightforward. The man's problem is to learn, to internalize what he has learned, and to teach others. His concern is to show the truth, to produce, to support, to facilitate, to beautify, to improve. The 3-hour movie passes so much that you laugh, you grieve, the movie ends. But you don't want the movie to end, you want more. This movie should definitely be watched.

Aamir Khan, Madhavan, and Sharman Joshi deliver stellar performances, making their characters feel real and relatable. With its inspiring message about following passion over societal pressure, 3 Idiots remains an iconic and timeless film that continues to resonate with audiences worldwide.

My first thought on this movie is mediocre due to the inconsistency at the first scene when they show a different plane for the same scene. This difference is quite visible because of the big difference in size of the two planes. As the story builds up and starts with the 3 character's first time meeting in the past at their old university. Keep in mind that this "past" is the memory of one of three main characters at the beginning. For a normal view, this is a good way to start a flashback in movies, novels, or other kinds of story. The memory is very good and precise.The plot that takes us back in time can make us forget that most of the film is just a memory of the character and his three best friends. The plot that immerses us in the really interesting stories of these three friends in their struggles in the world of lectures. This story has a solid plot and problems that are quite difficult to guess with the addition of comedy that makes us laugh and solving problems that can make us sad and moved. The emotions, reactions, and attitudes of these three idiot friends in dealing with the problems they face in unexpected ways are quite surprising and funny.The problems shown in 3 Idiots are real. Right from the parenting pressure part until the due-to-stress suicide part. The main characters depict all of the struggles they have met exquisitely. They criticized the strict education systems with their thought-provoking yet funny actions. Review Title: Great movie should definitely be watched
Review Text: At the start, i felt like a 3 hour movie will get boring for me but i was seriously surprised. I think this might be in the all time best movies ever made by bollywood. The story is based on light comedy but serious story is being told behind it. My personal opinion is that first half is a lot lighter then the second as the story begins to unfold itself on the audience in the second half. The story itself is very immersive and funny but sad at the same time which makes it an amazing movie to watch. The story is about 3 college friends, hence the name "3 idiots" and is a 3 hour long packed with laughable stupidity and serious heat touching moments. The lead cast includes Aamir Khan, Madhavan, Mona Singh and they played the roles beautifully. In my opinion, its a must watch movie as it won't disappoint. I myself would definitely watch it again someday.

Review Title: The best Indian Movie of all time.
Review Text: It has to be the most idiotic start to such a fascinating movie. You wonder where the movie is heading to when an airplane returns to base after a passenger(R.Madhavan) suffers a heart attack in an infirm manner. But Rajkumar Hirani soon gets his act and cast together to deliver a stunning product. Education is not about success; it's about pursuing excellence and doing what you are passionate about. In the end, success will chase you. This oft-repeated dialogue is beautifully portrayed in the climax. I have never laughed so hard before leaving a theater and you surely don't feel as a fourth idiot in the theater. Take a bow Mr Hirani. 3 IDIOTS is a fantastic film, one that every parent, teacher and most importantly students will dig into.'All Is Well', will soon be a craze across the nation. Hirani this time looks at the education system. The respectable, mechanical ways in which education is imparted where students are like machines. The setting is a prestigious engineering college and at the center of it all is Aamir Khan, a genius student who likes to see some fun injected in the lectures. Hirani touches on student suicides; parents pushing students into a field they are not interested ; students silently trying to excel in something they are not passionate about and also human emotions of how when a friend fails, you feel sad but when the same friend tops in college, jealousy takes hold. All these aspects and more are beautifully woven into the screenplay.There is a twist too, to spice up things at the interval. To get to the story, Farhan Qureshi (R Madhavan) Raju Rastogi (Sharman Joshi) and Rancho (Aamir Khan) are three united buddies. The film starts with Farhan and Raju getting some info on Rancho who has gone missing after their graduation. It's been (5-10) years now. It then moves to flashback. Where did Rancho disappear? Who was he really and what is he up to now? These questions keep you intrigued throughout the movie. The music by Shantanu Moitra is excellent and dialogue's by Hirani and Abhijit Joshi very inspiring. Although Hirani does not take pains to establish the friendship between the three, you overlook that fact because there is lot of meat in the screenplay. The film has been inspired from Chetan Bhagat's novel Five Point Someone. To knock off almost 20 years and pass off as a 24-year-old is a herculean task, one, which the diminutive Khan does beautifully.I mean, you do not see any persona of the Aamir of old in any angle, in any shot, in any dialogue delivery. A complete, brand new Aamir. You look for flaws but find none. Truly, a mark of a genius. The odd way in which he walks with his feet slightly apart is a delight. Sharman Joshi is excellent too. Madhavan could have taken his role more seriously and tried to fit in as a college student. Kareena, though does not have much of a role makes an impact while Mona Singh who makes her big screen debut does not get enough scope to show her talent. The one who steals the show with Aamir is Omi, the over zealous student who mugs his way to the top. This character is simply hilarious. In fact, it's a serious focus on how most students actually are today. Boman Irani as the college head for once is not loud and over-the-top. A clever make-up and twist of the tongue is what sets him apart from his other roles, which have become monotonous. There are many scenes that will grip you and others that will have you laughing silly. Like the last scene and the scene at Teacher's Day when Rancho changes the script.

'3 Idiots' is an all time favourite Bollywood movie especially in 2009. This film is a comedy movie with strong acting, memorable characters, and most importantly, highly motivational movie to choose the right path in your life.The producers of the film revealed some stunning views of the mountainous landscape of Ladakh. This scene was shot with a variety of different camera angles such as high angle, crane, and parallax shots. This colorful scene is cleverly used to infer that happy and joyful moments are coming. I found this film very educational as it tries to spread awareness and teach viewers about the increasing risk of suicide related to the school system in India. The mood of the film changes quickly from happy to sad to comedy etc. By doing so, it made the film more interesting because I was always on the edge of my seat not knowing what interesting events were going to happen. I used to think all Bollywood films were low budget with mean actors, but now I realize how incredibly convincing some of their actors are, even by Hollywood standards! The acting skills I witnessed gave me a great example of how amazing Bollywood films are and made me curious to watch more.Overall, '3 Idiots' was an amazing film that really changes the perspective of how good Bollywood films can be. All this time, I thought all Bollywood movies were cliche and not that tempting, but they proved me wrong. I mainly recommend it to those who want to start watching Bollywood or engineering students who are going through a lot lately.So grab some popcorn and enjoy the ride!

'3 Idiots (2009)' is the first proper Bollywood movie I've seen, even though I have seen the Hindi-language 'The Lunchbox (2013)', and it seems to be a pretty good place to start considering its general popularity, acclaim and relatively brisk runtime of just under three hours. The film is basically a satirical criticism of India's education system and the pressure put on students to becomes engineers, doctors, and other high-paid professionals rather than follow their dreams. Via a mixture of slapstick silliness, sincere melodrama, surprising darkness, and sidelined yet satisfying romance, the feature keeps you entertained for its duration, shifting and changing over time to deliver its message while also definitely making you laugh and maybe making you cry. It feels a little long and it's a bit baggy on occasion, but it never truly stagnates and it's engaging for its majority. It's utterly ridiculous at times, yet also features several really serious moments that threaten to give you tonal whiplash yet somehow don't. Its light moments work better than its heavy ones, yet everything lands to some extent and the flick typically walks its tonal tightrope really confidently. It's genuinely funny at times, and it's really endearing overall. It frequently puts a smile on your face and occasionally brings a tear to your eye, somehow remaining effective even as it becomes incredibly sappy (which often backfires and feels somewhat condescending or artificial). It only features two full-blown musical numbers, plus one montage set to a song and a brief in-universe strum-and-sing, but these are catchy, vibrant and energetic sequences that are really charming. I almost wish it featured more musical moments, closer to a traditional musical, because these scenes are so colourful and compelling. As a whole, this is a really well-made, well-acted, and enjoyable experience that has something to say but doesn't let that overwhelm its main purpose: entertainment. It's a lot of fun overall, and it crafts an atmosphere that makes you more inclined to forgive its issues (including some narrative messiness, tonal inconsistencies, and a handful of slightly iffy jokes). It's a great time, and it makes me want to explore more of what Bollywood has to offer. It's honestly pretty fantastic for what it is, flaws and all (is well).

Review Title: The Unconventional Wisdom of Friendship and Dreams
Review Text: Watching "3 Idiots" was like embarking on a rollercoaster ride of emotions, laughter, and life lessons that linger long after the credits roll. From the moment the film began, I was drawn into the vibrant world of Indian engineering students, where the pressures of academic excellence clash with the pursuit of genuine passion and understanding.The film's narrative is woven beautifully through the lives of three friends, each representing different facets of the educational experience. Aamir Khan's portrayal of Rancho is both charismatic and inspiring, as he challenges the conventional norms of education with his unique perspective on learning. His infectious enthusiasm and unwavering belief in chasing one's dreams resonated deeply with me, reminding me of my own journey through academia.What struck me most was the film's ability to balance humor with poignant moments. I found myself laughing out loud during the hilarious bathroom dance sequence, yet equally moved by the more serious themes surrounding mental health and parental pressure. The film doesn't shy away from addressing the harsh realities faced by students, making it relatable to anyone who has ever felt overwhelmed by expectations.The cinematography is vibrant, capturing the essence of college life in India, while the music, though initially seeming average, perfectly complements the emotional beats of the story. I particularly enjoyed how the songs were seamlessly integrated into the narrative, enhancing rather than distracting from the plot.One of the standout aspects of "3 Idiots" is its memorable quotes and life lessons. Phrases like "All is well" and "Chase excellence, and success will follow" became mantras for me, encapsulating the film's core message about resilience and the importance of following one's passion.As the film reached its climax, I was taken aback by the unexpected twists that left me both shocked and reflective. It's a testament to the storytelling prowess of Rajkumar Hirani, who crafts a narrative that is not only entertaining but also thought-provoking.In conclusion, "3 Idiots" is more than just a film; it's an experience that encourages viewers to rethink their approach to education and life. It's a celebration of friendship, dreams, and the courage to be different. I walked away feeling inspired and uplifted, eager to share this gem with friends and family. If you haven't seen it yet, do yourself a favor and dive into this delightful journey-you won't regret it.

Review Title: One of my favorite movies of all time
Review Text: It's so strange reviewing this movie after almost 10 years of its release. But after watching it again for the 50th time (no joke) I knew I had to write a review. I remember watching this movie when it was first released in theaters. As an 11 year-old Indian- American my hindi was pretty bad and some of the comedic scenes such as the one where Chatur was giving the speech in front of his school and made a mess (which i consider one of the best comedic scenes in any movie) went right over my head. Even the central message of the film which was to not follow success, but to do what you love and that success would follow you, was something I had still yet to understand. But now, watching this movie as a 20 year-old college student, really resonated with me. The trials and tribulations of being successful to appease your parents while also still battling the true aspirations that reside within you. All I can end this review by saying is this movie is truly a masterpiece and sure it did have some ridicoulous moments and scenes which could have been done without. But it's essence is timeless and even 30 years later, kids and adults alike will connect with this film.

Aaamir Khan, the man never ceases to amaze you. His friendship changes their lives forever. But, somehow he goes missing after their college. Where did he go and can they able to find their friend back is the rest of the story.

Review Title: Don't be stupid, be an idiot!
Review Text: "3 Idiots" is a masterpiece of Indian cinema, directed by Rajkumar Hirani and starring Aamir Khan, Kareena Kapoor Khan, and R. Madhavan. Well, the movie is a perfect blend of comedy, drama, and social commentary, which makes it one of the greatest films of all time. It tells the story of three college friends, Rancho (Aamir Khan), Farhan (R. Madhavan), and Raju (Sharman Joshi), who are chasing their dreams in a prestigious engineering college. The movie is not just about the journey of these three friends, but it also highlights the flaws in the education system and the societal pressure to excel in academics.The film starts with a humorous scene where Farhan and Raju are searching for their lost friend Rancho, who is not interested in studies but is passionate about learning. The movie takes us on a journey where we see how Rancho's anomalistic methods of teaching and his unique perspective on life, changes the lives of his friends and the people around him. The film has a great mix of comedy and emotional scenes, which keeps the audience entertained throughout.For me, one of the most iconic scenes in the movie is the "Balatkar" speech given by Rancho. In this scene, Rancho exposes the lacks in the education system and questions the relevance of cramming knowledge without understanding it. The scene is not only entertaining but also highlights a serious issue that exists in the Indian education system. Another scene that stands out is when Rancho teaches Raju's paralyzed father to walk. The emotional scene shows the power of determination and the importance of not giving up.The movie's soundtrack is also exceptional, with songs like "All Izz Well" and "Give Me Some Sunshine" becoming instant hits. The songs are both catchy and meaningful, adding to the emotional depth of the movie. The performances by the cast are outstanding, with Aamir Khan stealing the show as Rancho. His portrayal of a carefree but intelligent student is flawless. R. Madhavan and Sharman Joshi also deliver excellent performances as Farhan and Raju, respectively. Kareena Kapoor Khan plays the love interest of the three friends, and her chemistry with Aamir Khan is charming.Moreover, "3 Idiots" is a must-watch movie that will make you laugh, cry and think. It is a movie that will stay with you long after you have watched it. The plot was filled with light drama, intelligent humor, and surprising twists that give the audience a variety of events that capture their intellectual and emotional quotients. It circles into drama, comedy, romance, and even action. The plot relates to the audience because it rouses their minds to sympathize and empathize with the characters in the story and to ponder the message that it portrays at every turn. The film highlights important issues in the education system and the social pressure to excel academically, making it a movie that everyone should watch. Aside from the emotional whiplash, 3 Idiots will be meaningful for anyone who has ever struggled to find their bliss.

Review Title: One of The Best Things I've Got in 2021
Review Text: Let's start with the title. When I saw the title, the first thing I thought in my mind was that this movie will be bored and I am not interested at first. But I decided to watch this movie because it was the recommendation from my teacher at school.There were lots of scene taught me that life is not always about competition and compete. If there is a chance to study, we can learn from many things, not just from the book. There was a scene that made me realized: when Rancho gave advise to Raju that "we'll study with all our hearts, not just for grades. Follow excellence and success will chase you", It changed my point of view, because all this time I always push myself so hard to get a perfect grade, and sometimes I feel exhausted. So, I got a good lesson for build up my identity and my spirit of study.I really admire for Rancho, Farhan, and Raju's friendship. Through the good times and bad times, they have commitment to be loyal and care to each other. Although they separated for many years, but they get along with each other again.As for the plot, it's very long and involved and I could recount what occurs, but the plot was really entertaining and relate to our daily lives. It is an extremely well-made film about doing what you love and facing your fears. The acting is incredible by the ensemble cast. The script is funny and poignant at the same time. The film was a great one, although it's long in duration, it has the flavor of a comedy at the same time we are learning in every scenario. It gave an impact to every college life, and on how learning is about.First thing first is the friendship they build together. As they took their first step being a freshmen student, they instantly find themselves a companion. The film taught us that real friends would really help you up in times of difficulties. Forced learning and learning with passion, these two are totally different. The film portrays the two possible sides of a student why they took up Engineering. Some students were forced to study Engineering because of the pressure from the family so they would end up being afraid to fail during a test and the worst case is they would commit suicide. On the other side, learning with passion students took up engineering because it's their own desire, no one is forcing them to do it and so they were mostly on top. This taught us that we should follow what we desire, what we want and what we love. Being forced to something that is not our dream is like depriving us to pursue the future we want. The three characters show us the different characteristics of a student. Farhan took up engineering because he was forced but in the end, he chose to be a wildlife photographer and it was a very touching scene how he confessed to his father and being accepted. Raju took up engineering because he has a purpose to help his poor family. Lastly, Rancho is the most optimistic one, he took engineering because the hit is his passion and his dream. Yet, the pressure is a murderer. Another scheme of the film which also strikes me is the pressure they gave towards the students. The system is obliged to focus on the excellence of students. College is supposed to mold the students to prepare for the future and not pressure them to get high grades. Then again, the other side shows positivity. In the film, Rancho keeps on developing his knowledge and uses his skills in helping other people in need. He contributes to saving people and inspired them to strive more. It was an inspiring one.When we feel that we are in a verge of failings on what we are doing, we should need to look back on why did we took up this program, look back to the people we want to lift up once we are done and look back to our desired dreams and use all of these to make us feel motivated again. Failure is not forever, we all need to be a failure before we condemn as a winner. So what if we fail? it doesn't make us less, Instead, it makes us be wiser in making such decisions in the future. The film clearly depicts the struggles and the pressure of college students. As an engineering student, the film serves us with what we need to understand. It's about choosing happiness at the same time pursuing your dreams. As it said, "making your profession into a passion". We may have different reasons why we are pursuing this as long as we find the bliss of learning not because we are forced to. Watch the film and you might learn more than what I learned.

It is incredible how it shows serious matters in a light manner as well. Great acting, interesting insight into Indian culture and schooling.
