3 Idiots is an Indian comedy film directed by Rajkumar Hirani. This film was based on a novel called 'Five Point Someone' that was written by an Indian author, Chetan Bhagat. This film was released in 2009 and became a success as it became one of the most popular films worldwide at that time.3 Idiots tells us a story of three buddies named Rancho, Farhan, and Raju who attend the Imperial College of Engineering, an elite college in India. This film conveys and judges the educational system of India. Aamir Khan played Rancho in the role of a genius and motivational student. Ranganathan Madhayan played Farhan who was forced by his strict father to pursue engineering but has a dream to become a wildlife photographer. Sharman Joshi as Raju who is pursuing engineering to get his family out of poverty but is struggling to keep up with uni-life. Another character who played a big role in this film is their extremely-strict dean, Viru Sahastrabuddhe who was played by Boman Irani.I don't find myself watching Bollywood films that much. Especially comedy. I was kinda doubtful before watching this film even though my friend said it's one of the best films she has ever watched. A long 3 hours of comedy just didn't sit right with me- That was before I gave it a try to watch this. Never thought that this could turn out so good. It didn't start that well for me- but getting into the film for an hour, it was starting to get so entertaining and interesting. 3 idiots had success to make these 3 hours feel like nothing for me. The film; no doubt- leaves a great impression on me. I love how smooth the storyline goes. I never get confused when the timeline changes from ten years ago to the present. I experienced such a roller coaster of emotion watching this film. . Maybe because it is a Bollywood film, I didn't expect any particular scenes so the storyline for me wasn't predictable. All the humorous situations are present but then the story pulls back with a serious situation; poignant moments which could get you on nerves or even cry. This film was so nicely made; thanks to the wonderful script and the outstanding acting of the cast.This film surely tells you about the meaning of life; also a wholesome and meaningful friendship between these 3 'idiots'. The message in this film- I don't find anything new, I've been hearing from here and there that big grades don't mean success. You gotta believe in yourself, do your passion, and don't let others choose your life path. I'm always on the same page with this quote-unquote life advice. But this film opened my eyes wider and wider. It was because of the twist at the end of the film. Revealing the identity of Rancho. He proves that all he has been saying to his friends and teacher throughout the story is right; don't chase success. Achieve excellence and it will come to you itself. He proved it in a way that I didn't expect, I was shocked in fact. I'm not going to say the details and spoils each scene that was memorable for me so you better watch this film for yourself at least once. Just bring yourself to watch it. I bet you'll have a great time with all the comedies and twists that you'll hardly expect.

Review Title: Best movie in Bollywood industry so far
Review Text:
First I thought it is comedy movie. But after watching this movie I came to understand that it is a great mixture of comedy plus suspense. This movie actually tells about the lives of the students and the difficulties they face during college and university life. i think students should clear their concepts rather than cramming because if you want success in market you should adopt thoughtful learning and the role of the teacher is also vital. Teachers should be corporative with students. In this movie, when student asked to principal to check his project but principal didn't even see his project that makes student demotivated eventually he suicide. i think this is the most harsh scene in this movie.
As a student it is our duty to promote education and we should help needy students financially so that they will also take part in development of country and its excellence. In this movie Amir Khan pay money to the poor children to start it school so these are the main points of this movie.

Review Title: best movie to showcase thw mental pressure of a student
Review Text:
3 Idiots is an Indian comedy-drama surrounding the lives of three College friends, Farhan, Raju and Rancho. It is loosely adapted from the novel Five Point Someone by Chetan Bhagat.The film starts some years after the trio have graduated, but Rancho has somehow vanished along the way. In the exciting opening sequences, word emerges of Rancho's whereabouts. Raju runs around pants-less and Farhan pulls a stunt worthy of Hollywood on an airplane as they charge off hot on the trail of their missing friend.The film then follows the three friends from when they first met at the residences of the Imperial College of Engineering. In between, they get up to the usual mischief. The film explains that each of the trio is studying engineering for different reasons. Farhan is studying engineering to fulfil his family's wishes; Raju comes from a poor family and being an engineer will pull his family from poverty; and Rancho is studying because he is passionate about engineering. This sets the stage for the plot later on.
:At College, they cross paths with various characters. The Dean of the College, Professor Viru "Virus" Sahastrabuddhe is an overachiever, having taught himself to write with both hands to save time, among other skills. He has his own idea of the perfect student and routinely patronizes students who do not meet his criteria. This comes down to most of the students at the College. Fellow student, Chatur "Silencer" Ramalingam, provides the comedic element of the film. Chatur is ambitious and believes that textbook memorization of engineering concepts is the way to success. He considers himself superior to others at the university, taking shortcuts to impress the Dean. His efforts often make him the lot of jokes and pranks among the trio, one of which earns him the nickname "Silencer". Of course, no film is complete without romance, and this is provided by the appearance of Pia, Professor's Virus beautiful and intelligent daughter, with whom Rancho falls in love.In the middle of all these, they crash a wedding, deliver a baby on a makeshift operating table, settle a bet, fall in love and have hopes and dreams broken, all in Bollywood style. The film draws on themes of filial piety, obedience, friendship and the importance of following your heart. It also subtly hints that perhaps while we study, not all of us learn. The trio live, laugh and love with each other while discovering more about themselves. As usual, there is dancing, music, drama, a catchy phrase and soaring breath-taking scenery. The film ends with several twists and leaves you inspired to take a closer look at your life.

This movie has a lot. It is a comedy, but it is also tearjerking. It is really clever but also has its fair share of fart jokes. It shows the importance of education while highlighting the flaws of schooling. Sometimes it is a musical, and sometimes it is black and white. Its runtime may be nearly 3 hours, but it fills it up with a variety.The friendship of the three main characters is so charming. Rancho's simple but profound way of looking at things is effortlessly endearing. The film helps show that your happiness is not dictated by your income, and grades do not determine your intelligence.On the negative side, there are some frustrating character decisions that are... well, idiotic. The way in which a character named "Virus" is portrayed so much as an over-the-top strawman, he is just the worst. Sometimes it can be comedic, but a lot of times it is too much. And there is just two too many scenes of people peeing on things.Overall, this is a great film. It is profound without being pretentious. And it is optimistic without feeling fake.

Review Title: being an idiot isnt too bad
Review Text: Sometimes life gets too heavy to bear. Dealing with social pressure, as people keep on having high expectations on grades, marks, and how society portrayed success as to be wealthy. The movie '3 Idiots' elucidates it all. It will make your eyes open on how to walk through a path of life with a greater perspective. This movie is wrapped with a peak of comedy and slice of life.'3 Idiots' is a story about three engineering college students who have a hard time struggling through college. Farhan, the one who takes engineering because of his father's demands. Raju, who takes engineering so he could help his family from poverty. On the other hand, Rancho likes engineering, and as he lives with a different and positive point of view on how life works. "Life is not all about grades but chasing your real dream". At the end of the movie, all three of them have their path, as they pursue their dream job.I was not expecting much from this movie, but instead, this movie gives me a new point of view of life. 3 Idiots is a simple movie yet entertaining to watch. The comedy and the meaning of this movie is extraordinary. Every scene from the movie captivates me with each of it turns despite the rather lengthy duration of the movie. As the ending credits roll, I can't help but to agree on the movie's claims towards nowaday's education system.This Movie satisfies its comedic, thoughtful, and touching purposes. They spice up the movie with in-between songs and peak comedy lines that keeps the audience alive and yearning to know more about these 3 idiots. Though the purpose is conveyed boldly of how Rancho views the education system as flawed, it is stated constantly and persistently throughout the movie. This engages the audience to actively think and criticize the system as well. They're very clever in hitting the audience's soft spot by providing depressing truths of the main character's life and therefore creating attachment between the audience and the main characters. As the character cries and mourns, the audience will certainly feel their heart ache in sync.Taking everything into account, 3 idiots is a versatile movie that any age group can enjoy, laugh, and relate to. They bravely announce the faults that we as humans struggle with, the education system, and wrap it up with the catchphrase "All is well". It is peculiar how every audience will certainly relate to 3 engineering students and have their views of life changed after watching their story. This movie is worth our 2 hours and 51 minutes as it is easily life changing.

It is peculiar how every audience will certainly relate to 3 engineering students and have their views of life changed after watching their story. This movie is worth our 2 hours and 51 minutes as it is easily life changing.

Review Title: This movie has changed my mind about Indian film!
Review Text: At first, I was prejudiced with Indian films because people around me often say that Indian movies are a low quality movie and they don't like that in every movie there must be an interleaved song throughout the movie. But after I try to open up my heart and watch this movie, it made me realize that Indian movies are just as good as other countries! Even though there are interrupted songs in the movie, I think this is a very good and catchy song. I must admire Aamir Khan who is both the director and the main actor. He was able to create a really good movie. It is a movie that has all tastes - happiness, sadness, funny, and also hidden with insights throughout the story. Which we can understand and feel into it, because it is a story that we can meet in real life such as competitive exams, the expectations of the people around you, and the goals that must be reached."All is well" is my favorite phrase from this movie. It's a very powerful phrase! 'Whenever you become afraid or lack confidence in yourself, please tell yourself with this sentence.' This is one of the things I get from the movie. It is considered one of the most important scenes in the movie and the music (All Izz Well) of this scene is a song that many people like. I think that people, who have been prejudiced with the music of Indian movies, will changed their mind after watched this scene as I changed my mind.Aamir Khan is amazing person. He can create movies that make our emotions fell into the movie. Besides this movie, he also has a number of films that are equally excellent. It is worth watching!

The film begins with the entry of our threesome in the city's elite engineering college. It takes the first tryst with the mandatory ragging sessions which enunciate who the leader of the gang is going to be: new entrant Baba Rancchoddas, as his friends fondly call him. Rancho not only leads his friends through the maze of India's competitive, high-pressure, rote-heavy, illogical and almost cruel education system, he tutors them on several life mantras too. Like, running after excellence, not success; questioning not blindly accepting givens; inventing and experimenting in lieu of copying and cramming; and essentially following your heart's calling if you truly want to make a difference.

When I was a kid, everyone in my class had watched 3 Idiots. They all used to laugh at me for some reason because I didn't watch it. So the day this movie featured on TV, I watched it. And I have seen it every single time it comes on TV. And afterwards I began laughing at them because I had seen it more number of times than them. But well, immaturity aside, let's review this movie.The film talks about the rat race that everyone goes through, that we have been brainwashed into believing ever since we were teenagers. They always tell us that we need to get into a good engineering college if we want to have a good job irrespective of which field we work in, then that will play out when we have to get married, then we will have children and then the cycle will go on for them. We don't dare to think otherwise, because the elders always have a sense of insecurity about us and also because they always resort to shouting and scolding whenever we dare to question their authority.The film has become something of a synonym with engineering life. Everyone learnt about the various things that happen in the top engineering college, because I think this is one of the first commercially successful Bollywood films to talk about the environment. There are three protagonists, each being a stereotype of an engineering student we all can relate to - Rancho the brilliant guy who doesn't study much but still gets good marks because he loves what he's doing, Raju who is a superstitious and cowardly guy coming from a poor household, and Farhan, a student who really has a passion for photography but his parents are forcing him to do engineering. Basically the three types of engineering students we all know.Together they spend their college life in fun ways, teaching each other the meaning of life and the points of views of each person. And then after they leave college, they all decide to meet each other because of a "challenge" that Chatur, a teacher's pet had given them. Chatur wasn't exactly wrong from his point of view, since he came from Uganda, a country where Indians were being persecuted back in the late 20th century. He had learnt early in life that one has to take all means possible in order to achieve success, and that's exactly what he was going with.Every time I watch this film I end up managing to find a new detail that I had previously missed. I watch it every time it comes on TV because of that reason. It was only later that I got to know this is actually a book adaptation of Five Point Someone by Chetan Bhagat, and those friends of mine who laughed at me for not having seen this film are the same people who ridicule Chetan Bhagat for his terrible books. And to be honest they are kind of right, since this is his only good book adaptation. And by good I mean absolutely flawless.

To excel in your career, one needs to be interested, confident and excited about the selected career and should believe in oneself and talent. The movie contains some adult contents which are inappropriate. This movie also promotes suicide attempt in order to get rid of troubles, hardships and pressure of education which is not a good way to get rid of difficulties of life. Overall, it is an amazing movie that has a lot of messages for the society in which we are surviving and is worth watching.

Review Title: Its Aamir!! Aamir!! Aamir!! And a lovely, enjoyable screenplay
Review Text: Now, I MUST admit that I am not a north Indian. I don't understand Hindi and I am actually not an ardent fan of Aamir Khan. But after watching this movie, I just admire him so much that I can't even explain it. See his body language all over this movie. He may not have as much as acting scope as in many previous movies like Mangal Pandey, Lagaan, Gajini and all. But still, He just turned out to be a teenage student in this movie.Watch his body language all over the film. See how we wears his dress. You just can't point his "ACTUAL" age. Before watching this movie, I had a doubt, how could a 44 year old "MAN" can act like a teen age "GUY". But he just proved that "HE" can. Being the oldest among the three leads, he makes feel like the youngest.The scene when he elaborates "MACHINES"; I just felt like watching Rowan Atkinson. How can a man act both like Rowan Atkinson in his latest movie and like an action hero like Al Pacino in his previous film (Ghajini). He's just irreplaceable.And the movie's screenplay, Wow!!!!! I should admit that There is still 20 minutes left to watch. But I just couldn't be patient to write a review for such a lovely movie. It is just Laugh Out Loud Movie. I am not Hindi guy and still I can laugh at many places. Watching with subtitles and still not every dialog's are loading. Still I am Rolling in floor. One thing that squeezes my mind is that, how can a commercial action hero do such a comical role without any care about his "IMAGE"? I agree, Akshay has already done it, but still, Isn't Aamir in a much more higher position than Akshay? (Am ignorant about Bollywood. So please adjust with it) Madhavan & Joshi had a very little scope in this movie I suppose. Yes, I still have 20 minutes left to watch. But I don't think that, this finishing 20 minutes will change my view from the previous 2:20 hours of the movie. This movie is planned to be remade in Tamil (my mother tongue). Sidharth of 'Striker' fame is doing Maddy's role. I don't think he would make some fortune with this character. But it IS sure, that Vijay of Tamil 'Pokiri' fame (which Salman remade in Hindi as "Wanted") who is doing Aamir's role, will hold back his Star value in Tamil Cinema after giving continuous flops.Joshi's role is going to be done by Jeeva who is really a promising upcoming artist. He will be outperformed by Vijay in this movie which can be guaranteed.Well, Its time for me to watch the remaining 20 minutes and enjoy.

Review Title: Satire-Comedy Bollywood Movie, 3 Idiots Can Change your Perspective.
Review Text:
Adapted from Chetan Bhagat's humour novel titled Five Point Someone, 3 Idiots made a big hit in the Bollywood world. After hit movies Taare Zameen Par and Ghajini, Aamir Khan made his appearance as the lead actor starring as Rancho. Also, R. Madhavan as Farhan and Sharman Josi as Raju. The friendship journey of three engineering college students tells a satire about the social pressures under Indian educational system. The three of them had a great chemistry and left as memorable characters with their strong acting. Their performance changed my perspective about 'usually cheap' Bollywood films. The acting abilities are mind-blown and it intrigues me to watch more. Produced by Vindu Vinod Chapre and directed by Rajkumar Hirani, this film is narrated through parallel drama, in the present and ten years in the past. 3 Idiots is breath-takingly amazing, containing the beautiful landscape of Ladakh through various different angles.This movie has a good plot that I really did not expect. Perhaps it is because there are lots of funny moments, so I did not think too much just laughing out loud. And the plot packed nicely, one by one the truth was revealed with a comedy interlude which is basically a comedy film that was unthinkable at all. This film serves various moods such as joyous funny comedy to depressing. The disposition of the characters is well-written, all the characters have their own iconic habits. This film taught us many valuable lessons. And also there were many things related to real life, like family problems, parenting problems, college student problems, educational problems, dreams, passion, friendship, and how to deal with those problems. This is a compilation of my personal favorite quote that I want to share: "You don't need to be afraid to be yourself, what your heart likes to do just do it because life's opportunity doesn't come twice. The future is full of mystery, but you don't need to be afraid to face it, stay optimistic and positive thinking"
Overall, it is a must-watch and leaves a huge motivation to choose a path in life. It impressed me about how great a Bollywood film can be. Going from jubilant to sad in a split second, this film was such a roller-coaster experience. There were so many valuable scenes, that I even cried twice during the film. 3 idiots was just a perfect combination of educational and spreading awareness about academic related suicide risk in India covered with enough comedy. I can say that this is one of the best Bollywood film I have ever watched, 9/10 from me.

Review Title: Three Idiots - A Heartwarming and Hilarious Journey of Friendship and Self-Discovery
Review Text: Three Idiots (2009), directed by Rajkumar Hirani, is a cinematic gem that blends humor, heart, and thought-provoking commentary on the pressures of modern education. Your 8/10 rating highlights the film's ability to entertain and provoke deeper reflections about the pursuit of success, happiness, and self-identity. This Bollywood classic is both a social commentary and a celebration of friendship, with unforgettable characters and a narrative that resonates far beyond the confines of Indian culture.At its core, Three Idiots is about three engineering students-Rancho (Aamir Khan), Farhan (R. Madhavan), and Raju (Sharman Joshi)-whose friendship helps them navigate the intense pressures of their academic lives. The film begins with a reunion of the trio years after their college days, setting off a series of flashbacks that explore their time in college and their journey of personal growth, filled with laughter, tears, and moments of profound realization.The performances in Three Idiots are one of its most striking elements. Aamir Khan, as the free-spirited, unconventional Rancho, delivers a performance that is both charismatic and heartwarming. His character challenges the status quo, advocating for learning through understanding rather than rote memorization. Khan brings both humor and wisdom to the role, making Rancho an inspiring figure without ever feeling preachy. His portrayal of Rancho's idealism and rebellious nature against the rigid educational system strikes a chord with audiences, making him a standout in a film that is driven by emotional and intellectual depth.R. Madhavan as Farhan, the aspiring wildlife photographer stuck in a career his parents forced upon him, and Sharman Joshi as Raju, the anxious and struggling student battling family pressures, both give outstanding performances that capture the pain and vulnerability of students stuck in a system that doesn't prioritize individuality. The bond they share with Rancho provides the emotional backbone of the film, and their characters' development throughout the story is both uplifting and emotionally resonant.The humor in Three Idiots is another aspect that elevates it above a typical drama. The film expertly balances light-hearted comedic moments with serious themes. Whether it's the witty dialogue, slapstick moments, or hilarious situations that the characters find themselves in, the film never loses its charm. The comedy serves not only to entertain but also to make serious points about the pressures students face in their academic journeys. The dynamic between the three leads is electric, and the camaraderie they share feels genuine and relatable.The film's direction by Rajkumar Hirani is masterful. His trademark storytelling style blends humor with social commentary in a way that feels natural and deeply engaging. The narrative moves between past and present seamlessly, using the structure of a mystery-what happened to Rancho after graduation-to keep the audience hooked while exploring the trio's transformation. Hirani's skillful use of flashbacks and parallel storytelling heightens the emotional impact of the film, drawing the audience into the characters' lives and struggles.Visually, Three Idiots is vibrant and energetic. Cinematographer C. K. Muraleedharan captures the sweeping landscapes of India with a sense of grandeur, as well as the intimacy of the characters' emotional journeys. The film takes us from the chaotic campus life to the vast openness of the outdoors, emphasizing the contrast between the confined pressures of the educational system and the boundless possibilities of following one's passion.The film's music, composed by Shantanu Moitra, is equally memorable. The songs, including "All Is Well" and "Give Me Some Sunshine," are uplifting and catchy, serving as anthems of defiance against the oppressive educational system. The music adds to the film's energy and perfectly complements its themes of freedom and self-discovery.One of the most powerful aspects of Three Idiots is its critique of the traditional educational system. It questions the emphasis placed on grades, degrees, and standardized tests at the cost of creativity, critical thinking, and mental well-being. The film's central message is simple yet profound: follow your passion, learn with curiosity, and success will follow. It challenges the toxic culture of academic competition and urges young people to find their own paths, free from the fear of failure and societal expectations.The film also addresses important themes of parental pressure and the societal expectation of success. Farhan and Raju's struggles with their parents' dreams for them are poignant and relatable. Their journeys toward breaking free from those pressures provide some of the most emotional moments of the film. The contrast between the characters' personal growth and the rigid system they are fighting against is where the film truly excels.The only reason your 8/10 rating might not be a 10 is that at times, the film does lean into melodrama, and some might find the pacing a bit predictable in certain places. However, these minor flaws are easily overshadowed by the film's overall emotional impact and cultural relevance.In conclusion, Three Idiots is a remarkable film that combines humor, heartfelt drama, and important social commentary about the educational system. Its unforgettable performances, engaging storyline, and thought-provoking themes make it a standout in Indian cinema and a film that resonates with audiences worldwide. With a perfect mix of comedy, emotion, and life lessons, Three Idiots proves to be much more than just an entertaining movie-it's a celebration of individuality, friendship, and the pursuit of happiness.

This is one of my favourite films ever.It follows the lives of three friends from university, their struggles with family, class, societal expectations, and educational demands in Indian culture. It was based on a book I think.It's a comedy and a drama and a bromance film. It has a message, but it is also uplifting.The songs are on point, and the musical numbers add to the story, add some zest, without being annoying or distracting (as in some musicals I've seen).There are some intense, sad scenes, and hilarious ones. The friendship of the three guys is the heart of the story, with messages of following your dreams, hope, loyalty.TW suicide.

Like so many Indian films, this is a very, very long film--with a run-time of almost three hours. When a movie is bad or just okay, this can seem like forever, but since "3 Idiots" is a very, very good film I loved its length. And, like most films of the genre, it has its share of the usual singing and dancing so foreign to films from other countries. One thing you should know, however, is that defining the type of film it is isn't really easy. Much of it is a comedy, but it also has many poignant moments (keep the Kleenex nearby), some existential moments where they explore the meaning of life and work and it's also a tender film about friendship. And, as my daughter pointed out when she saw the film, she loved that the men in the movie are not afraid to cry--something you rarely see in western films.As for the plot, it's very long and involved and I could recount what occurs. But I don't want to spoil a single wonderful moment, so my advice is just sit back and watch--and if you give it a chance, I can almost guarantee you'll have a great time with this poignant and funny film. Wonderful and well worth your time--with a delightful script, wonderful characters and lots of moments that made me smile...and a few that brought me to tears. See this film.

Review Title: Perfect Blend of Heart, Humor, and Truth - Cinema at Its Finest
Review Text: 3 Idiots is my all-time favorite film-a flawless masterpiece that strikes a rare balance between laughter, emotion, and powerful social commentary. It tackles the pressures of academic life, the rigidity of the education system, and the fear of failure with both tenderness and courage.Rajkumar Hirani and Aamir Khan are at their absolute peak here. Every scene feels purposeful, every twist earned, and every moment unforgettable. The film doesn't just entertain-it inspires, challenges, and stays with you long after the credits roll.Few films can make you laugh, cry, and reflect all at once. 3 Idiots does it with ease. A timeless classic that deserves every bit of praise.

3 Idiots- 2009 Director: Rajkumar HiraniWhat can be expected from Amir Khan? QUALITY ! 3 idiots suits the term 'landmark cinema' and has all it takes to help Bollywood take a giant step into world cinema with pride. It is a sparkling example of qualitative cinema. 3 idiots not only entertains, it also enlightens the audience. First Bollywood film to reach 100 Crore Club. An Aamir Khan film is nothing short of an event. The supremely talented actor acts in one film a year and no two films are ever identical in terms of plot line. Much more can still be summarized about the film. Lovely!

I rate this film 9 I would rather rate it 9 than 10. However, something between 9 and 10 would have been a better indicator of my approval.It is certainly one of the most entertaining movies I have ever watched. This includes all Hindi, Bengali, and English movies (and movies in other languages) that I have watched, and I have watched a lot. Indeed, I would be hard put to mention another that I found to be as entertaining and I definitely cannot remember one that I found more delightful. The movie succeeds in all fronts--storyline, humour, sheer fun, pathos, visualization, imaginative elements, sound, music, editing, sense of balance, and overall inspiration. And, the actors have done their best. It would be unfair to single out any of them besides others. However, I cannot resist naming Omi Vaidya and Boman Irani. Hats off!

"3 Idiots" is a cinematic gem that takes you on an emotional rollercoaster while simultaneously provoking deep thoughts on the education system and societal expectations. Directed by Rajkumar Hirani, this 2009 Indian comedy-drama has stood the test of time, captivating audiences with its brilliant storytelling, stellar performances, and profound messages.Set against the backdrop of an elite engineering institute in India, the film follows the journey of three friends, Rancho (played by Aamir Khan), Farhan (R. Madhavan), and Raju (Sharman Joshi). "3 Idiots" seamlessly blends humor, drama, and social commentary, making it a unique and unforgettable cinematic experience.The screenplay, penned by Hirani and Abhijat Joshi, is a masterpiece in itself. It ingeniously weaves together the past and present, creating a narrative that keeps you engrossed from start to finish. The dialogues are witty, thought-provoking, and often laced with humor, making the film simultaneously entertaining and insightful.Aamir Khan's portrayal of Rancho is a standout performance, showcasing his versatility as an actor. He effortlessly brings life to the character, infusing it with charm, intelligence, and a rebellious spirit. The chemistry between the three lead actors is palpable, their camaraderie adding depth to the story. R. Madhavan and Sharman Joshi deliver commendable performances, perfectly balancing the comedic and emotional aspects of their roles."3 Idiots" tackles pressing issues in the education system, highlighting the obsession with grades, rote learning, and the lack of emphasis on true learning and individuality. The film encourages viewers to question societal norms and follow their passions, rather than succumbing to societal pressures. It presents a strong case for reevaluating the purpose of education, urging us to prioritize knowledge, creativity, and happiness over mere academic excellence.The film's cinematography and production design are top-notch. The picturesque landscapes of Ladakh and Shimla serve as breathtaking backdrops, enhancing the visual appeal of the narrative. The music by Shantanu Moitra is soul-stirring and perfectly complements the storytelling. Songs like "Give Me Some Sunshine" and "All is Well" have become anthems.

Review Title: MASTERPIECE !!! MUST_WATCH.... BEST EVER MOVIE BY Mr. PERFECTIONIST
Review Text: Being engineering student I can relate every situation. It is inspire from a novel Five Point Someone.Greatest,realistic, entertaining movie I love it. It is the story of 3 friends Farhan,Raju and Rancho. When three student enter the engineering college they faced ragging session but today we are enough lucky that their is no more ragging session and it is an illegal act. " What is machine ? " The definition of machine everyone in his class memorized the definition but they never thought that it can be said in very easy way only we have to know the concept and utilize it now in the movie Rancho said the answer " Anything that simplifies work and saves time is a machine. In a warm day, press a button and you get a blast of air-a fan, that's a machine. Speak to a friend miles away - the telephone, that's a machine. Compute millions in seconds - a calculator, a machine. We are surrounded with machines, from a pen's nib to a pants zip, all machines " . It is exactly the machine means. He guided his friends not only in educational way but also he gave some life mantras which is useful to live a live. " All izz well " - the song it doesn't mean everything will be well but from inside it gave a courage and strength to fight back in bad situation . Raju when he started to fight back against his fear he had reached his destiny that means dedication,hard work and if we control our fear and nervousness then we can reach out goal. Farhan was interested in photography but he was study engineering which doesn't give any interest to him from this we can say if you are interested in that course then only join otherwise not don't choose any option which is not make you interest and don't pressure your child choose according to you please give them chance to choose his or her(child) own interest course which he/she want. Rancho had an innovative mind he doesn't believe in only getting degree but he believed in getting lots of knowledge and applying them in real life. Practical knowledge so that we can apply in future.He always want to give answer in logical way . Another song give me some sunshine this song means lot I can relate every line.Once we grow up we want to have those childhood days where we enjoy our life happily without stress.Every parents want his or her child should score 90%marks then parent will gift watch otherwise stick but in reality marks are needed but if you can't score 90%then it doesn't mean you are useless we should always target 90%and we should also deep clear concept about the subject and have practical knowledge to utilize it in real life .It is an amazing movie it gave lots of message. I love it and please watch it

Review Title: Cast and filmed in India, plays on the theme that dads want their boys to become engineers.
Review Text: I came across this movie on DVD from my public library. With that title it brought to mind a couple of movies, "Our Idiot Brother" and "Dumb and Dumber." Now having watched it I will say it is nothing like either one of those. At almost 3 hours I found it hard watch in one sitting so I did not try. I watched it in 4 sessions.There are a couple of overlapping themes. One is that instead of choosing your life's work by how much money it might earn you, as dads in India tend to do, choose something you love and if you do it well the other things will come. And, somewhat humorously, when things aren't going according to expectations just say "All is well" and things just might turn out OK.The "three idiots" are young men enrolled in the engineering school. I suppose here the term "idiot" is applied to those who seemingly don't make the best choices. But they aren't idiots, just highly unconventional.The movie is very entertaining. Many of the customs are entertaining, like the habit men have of urinating in public which, my web search reveals, is factual. But the spoken language is strange, using both English and Hindi, often by a character in the same sentence.

Rajkumar Hirani's 3 Idiots is not just a film-it's a cultural phenomenon that continues to resonate with students, parents, and professionals across generations. Released in 2009, this Bollywood gem remains timeless for its powerful message about the flaws in the education system, its emphasis on following one's passion, and its brilliant mix of comedy, drama, and emotion.Loosely adapted from Chetan Bhagat's novel Five Point Someone, 3 Idiots follows the journey of three engineering students-Rancho (Aamir Khan), Farhan (R. Madhavan), and Raju (Sharman Joshi)-as they navigate the pressures of a rigid academic environment. The story unfolds through flashbacks, with Farhan and Raju setting out to find their long-lost friend Rancho, who changed their lives during their college years. What begins as a light-hearted search slowly turns into a deeper reflection on friendship, dreams, and the true meaning of education.Aamir Khan, once again, delivers an exceptional performance as Ranchoddas Shamaldas Chanchad, better known as Rancho. With his wide smile, calm demeanor, and unconventional thinking, he becomes the voice of reason in a system obsessed with grades and rote learning. Aamir perfectly portrays the innocence, brilliance, and rebellious spirit of Rancho, making him one of the most beloved characters in Hindi cinema.Madhavan as Farhan brings sincerity and warmth to his role. His inner conflict between fulfilling his father's expectations and pursuing wildlife photography feels deeply relatable. Sharman Joshi shines as Raju, the nervous yet lovable friend who comes from a struggling background. His emotional breakdowns, humorous moments, and transformation arc add depth to the trio's dynamic.Kareena Kapoor, as Pia, the spirited and intelligent medical student (and daughter of the college dean), adds both charm and substance. Her chemistry with Aamir is natural, and her character plays a key role in bridging personal and ideological conflicts.Boman Irani as Viru Sahastrabuddhe, or "Virus," the strict, unimaginative college director, is both humorous and intimidating. His portrayal of an authoritarian figure reflects the rigid mindset of many academic institutions that prioritize marks over innovation.

Why is this movie so beautiful? How can a film make you feel almost every emotion possible? It feels like it somehow has the answer to both questions.The first time I watched it was around 2010. My girlfriend at the time wanted me to see it, and I remember saying something like, "Why a Bollywood movie? What does that have to do with anything?" But after a while, I started to feel completely mesmerized. Even the songs were captivating. When the first song started, I remember thinking, "Here we go..." But the moment *Give Me Some Sunshine* played - a song that is still on my playlist and still gives me goosebumps every time I hear it - the spell was complete.Is there anything bad that can be said about this movie? I honestly don't know. I watched it again, and once again, I was completely enchanted.

Meh. While the theme of Indian education system has a lot of potential for a good story, it's not enough to make the experience particularly enjoyable. The largest problem in my opinion is the dialogue. Essentially all characters, though especially Rancho, speak exclusively in such a smarmy way that's meant to present them as smart and free-spirited but come off as incredibly pretentious, arrogant and often still factually wrong about whatever topic they are discussing. It somewhat reminds me of an average Reddit comment or the worst of The Big Bang Theory. It gets tiring about 10 minutes in, let alone almost 3 hours(!) into the the film's runtime. All of this is interspersed with endless kindergarten-level jokes about farts, pee and what not, except told by adult men.As to the story...it's fine. It does a fairly good job depicting the flaws in Indian society and the younger generation of main characters trying to obstrigillate and surpass them. Unfortunately due to the aforementioned problems with the film it's hard to take the message seriously. It becomes especially grating when a certain character attempts suicide. What should realistically be a major event with serious implications ends up being yet another anecdote in a long series of anecdotes, not to mention it's immediately followed up with a cheerful musical performance, as is typical in these films. The music is not bad, in fact it's quite enjoyable. However it would be easier to appreciate it if the entire film was structured as a musical; or if it was left out altogether.Overall, not an experience worth one's time. Just go watch something else.

Review Title: The most boring movie i ever had seen
Review Text: The reason I am saying this is that Bollywood often does not put enough effort into adapting or recreating ideas that come from Hollywood. In many cases, it feels like the movies simply copy the basic concept or storyline without adding much originality or creativity of their own. Because of this, the final result can seem less engaging or less well-developed compared to the original inspiration. Additionally, the plot in some of these movies may not be very strong or well-structured, which makes it harder for the audience to stay interested throughout the film. On top of that, the choice of the leading actor can sometimes feel mismatched for the role, which further affects the overall quality of the movie. When the story is weak and the main performance is not convincing, it can make the entire film feel less impactful and enjoyable for viewers.

Review Title: That will shift your mindset about learning and success.
Review Text: If you're looking for a film that strikes a perfect balance between comedy and drama while offering thoughtful social commentary, 3 Idiots is an excellent choice.It's no surprise that the film won three National Film Awards and six Filmfare Awards, gained widespread international recognition, and became one of the highest-grossing Indian films of its time. Like many of Aamir Khan's movie characters, his portrayal of Rancho here is breath of fresh air, inspiring you to step outside the box, think differently and not simply follow the crowd.As a student who was never a fan of rigid, traditional learning, Rancho's character resonated deeply with me.The story tells about three engineering students navigating their student life in a highly competitive famous college. Rancho (Aamir Khan) who loved practical learning and seen as "idiot" because of his approach to living a unique life with different mindset about learning which are completely different from others. Farhan who struggled with his studies because his true passion lies somewhere and was forced by his father to study engineering for the sake of a stable career. And Raju who had financial struggles with paralyzed father and was constantly reminded to succeed because his family invested all their savings in his education. This pressure leaves him constantly afraid of failure and making his difficult to truly enjoy studying and college life.The story will unfold when those three became roommates and Rancho taught Raju and Farhan his way of living life. He believed learning is not memorizing textbooks and should be creative, engaging and fun.As a chemical engineering student, I admit there are times when academic pressure pushes me to focus more on memorizing for exams than truly understanding the materials. During those moments, I rewatch this film as a reminder to stay curious and avoid falling into rigid systems that prioritize marks over genuine learning.From a technical standpoint, the film stands out in many ways. The performances feel natural and believable, especially Aamir Khan's portrayal of Rancho, which balances sharp intelligence with genuine emotional depth. The supporting cast adds authenticity and richness to the story and makes you resonate with them.What truly makes the film shine, however, is its message. It challenges rigid educational systems and emphasizes the value of pursuing excellence over simply chasing success. At the same time, it explores themes of fear, failure, and identity, showing how external pressures can shape our choices and sense of self.One of the most memorable lines for me is "Pursue excellence, and success will follow," perfectly captures the heart of the film. It encourages a shift away from result-driven thinking toward a deeper focus on learning, curiosity, and passion. There is my favorite scene in the film which highlights what you've learned can be applied in real-life situations where they helped a child birth with their engineering knowledge.If you think it might be boring, let me tell you...this is the kind of film that will have you laughing out loud until your belly hurt, pulling at your heartstrings with emotions, and leaving you with a genuine sense of warmth with their bonds of friendship. And yes, there's also surprising plot twist along the way with a tinge of mystery as well.In conclusion, 3 Idiots is an insightful and inspiring film that successfully combines entertainment with a powerful message. Overall, it stands as a must-watch film which will transforms the way one thinks about learning after watching.

Like Dead Poets Society but with Robin Williams as a student and some extra tonic and spice thrown in for good measure. Serious, sad and silly, often concurrently but overwhelmingly very, very funny and engaging, as engineering students at an Indian college end up teaching as much as they learn. Who could disagree with the distinction between education and learning while recognising the often sizable gulf that exists between the two after watching this. Who would dissuade those with aspiration and passion from pursuing their dreams and goals. Weighing in not too shy of three hours you'll be shocked how quickly the time flies as your attention is vacuumed up and your view of the world turned on its head, or inverted, depending what you've learned.

It's hard for me to review this film, as I have not seen a huge number of Indian films--probably no more than a couple dozen. Most of the ones I've seen were wonderfully entertaining but I am far from an expert on Bollywood. Because of this, I have a hard time knowing how good this film is relative to other films from this country. So, consider this when you read this review. This may be among the very best India has to offer or it just seems that way to me.Like so many Indian films, this is a very, very long film--with a run-time of almost three hours. When a movie is bad or just okay, this can seem like forever, but since "3 Idiots" is a very, very good film I loved its length. And, like most films of the genre, it has its share of the usual singing and dancing so foreign to films from other countries. One thing you should know, however, is that defining the type of film it is isn't really easy. Much of it is a comedy, but it also has many poignant moments (keep the Kleenex nearby), some existential moments where they explore the meaning of life and work and it's also a tender film about friendship. And, as my daughter pointed out when she saw the film, she loved that the men in the movie are not afraid to cry--something you rarely see in western films.As for the plot, it's very long and involved and I could recount what occurs. But I don't want to spoil a single wonderful moment, so my advice is just sit back and watch--and if you give it a chance, I can almost guarantee you'll have a great time with this poignant and funny film. Wonderful and well worth your time--with a delightful script, wonderful characters and lots of moments that made me smile...and a few that brought me to tears. See this film.

Review Title: Aal izz very, very, very, very, very Well
Review Text: "Whatever the problem in life is... just say to yourself 'Aal Izz Well'.. This wont solve your problems but it will give the courage to face it.." "Chase Excellence and success will follow".. " Life is not about getting marks, grades but chasing your dreams".. These are the golden rules which 3 IDIOTS teaches you in a very light and entertaining way.. The movie makes you laugh and in the process you learn many golden rules which can alter your life in a big manner...3 actors from the path-breaking blockbuster RANG DE BASANTI( Aamir, Sharman, Madhvan) team up together with Rajkumar Hirani.. It couldn't get bigger! Loosely based on blockbuster novel ' Five Point Someone ' by Chetan Bhagat the movie deals with the present education system in India that whether getting more marks and better grades is better than gaining knowledge and is mugging up everything more useful than understanding it..The movie has many hilarious scenes and everyone in the film form Aamir to Boman Irani to Omi Vaidya were superb in their roles.. Aamir has outdone himself.. Never in the film he has looked 44 year old... Film's music sounds mediocre but when viewed on the screen then the music sounds perfect.. Cinematography is awesome and so is the direction.. Raju Hirani's screenplay(you can easily ignore a few loose points) is a masterpiece and the film will achieve cult status for engineering students in a short period of time.. It has the soul which Five Point Someone had.. It is easily Aamir Khan's finest film to date.. The film's climax is fantabulous and is a shocker.. It takes everyone by surprise...To conclude 3 IDIOTS has the potential to break all records and sweep all awards.. Watch it... You will Love it...My Rating: 9/10Thanks and Regards

Review Title: easily the best Indian movie in 2009.... and one of the best ever
Review Text: I was lucky to see the first day show of 3 idiots on Thursday in Dubai. Well the title of this post says it all. The movie is an example of classic film making. brilliant and highly entertaining.Watch 3 idiots for a) amir khan's incredible acting b) intelligent and highly energatic screenplay & direction (Rajkumar Hirani has done it yet again!) c) amazing chemistry among actors, d) some of the best comedy scenes coming from Indian cinema in a long time and e) a heart warming story which keeps you spell binded right till the end.I would rate it as among the best from amir khan (which obviously means among the very best from bollywood. I would also give a lot of credit to the director rajkumar hirani, who after this movie (and his previous munna bhai series) should rank as among the top in bollywood too. 9/10.

Review Title: One of the better Bollywood movies I saw
Review Text: Because the movie had such a high rating on IMDb I was intrigued. Bollywood movies are not particularly on the top of my list I must admit. But I gave it a shot and I'm glad I did. It only has two scenes where they start singing and dancing so that was not too much. Why they start singing and dancing that I will never get. The story does not need that at all. The story itself was very pleasant to follow. Full of mixed emotions, happy ones and sad ones. The cast and the filming was great. All actors gave a good performance throughout the whole movie. Like in most American television series they want to give you a lesson in life and most of the time it makes me want to barf but in this Bollywood movie it works perfectly well. I had fun watching this and I get why I am not the only one. Certainly worth a watch.

Review Title: more than just a laugh...taught me great life lessons as well
Review Text: I'm from the Philippines. For years, I have always been curious about Indian cinema. I've seen clips of Indian films on cable TV and to be honest...I just don't get it. I don't get all the silly dancing and singing in almost all movies. I find it so corny and irrelevant to the story. But don't get me wrong, Philippine movies back in the 80's were also filled with corny singing and dancing. But we have gone past those and transitioned into movie-making based on good scripts...rather than cheesy story lines. When a friend of mine recommended this movie, I haven't really heard of it. I thought it was a Hollywood release but it wasn't. It was a Bollywood movie. He said it was really funny, so out of curiosity, I got hold of a copy. My first thought was that it was nothing but a silly story of 3 guys doing slapstick comedy that I have all seen before. When they started singing "All iz well.." I told myself... I knew it! Just what I thought.But as the movie progressed...I was amazed at the intelligence embedded in this movie. I was so impressed with the smart dialogues delivered by Amir Khan. It was so mature and thought-provoking. The life crisis all of them were in... the pressures at school..the pressures from society and the pressures from family as well... they rang so true. I was even at one time crying my heart out. I can't believe it! All the preconceived notions about a Bollywood movie were obliterated. I was so moved by this picture and I have no regrets seeing it.

Unlike many other Bollywood movies, 3 Idiots was enjoyable - at some points.Firstly, the film provides a useful message - do what you love rather than what someone else thinks you should do. It also has a clever and quite unpredictable plot which makes the film certainly watchable.However, the reason for this film's downfall is due to its comedic aspect. The jokes and comedy to this film is super immature and childish and wouldn't make 99% of adults laugh. I genuinely believe that if this film had little to no comedic aspects, or at least a less childish comedic aspect, and was a serious film it could be comparable to Good Will Hunting.Had the potential to be a global success but only succeeded domestically.

Review Title: Easily one of the most overrated films on IMDb
Review Text: The script is the biggest problem with this film. It is filled with clichés and unoriginal jokes. The theme and message of the film is spelled out for the audience a million times. It should be more subtle and realistic. The sequence of events is entirely predictable.Additionally, there is nothing exceptional about any other element of the film in my opinion. There is nothing impressive about the camera work. There is nothing impressive about set design or costume design. There is nothing impressive about the acting or characterization.It is emotionally manipulative using every trick in the book and there is nothing to enjoy if you do not fall for that. How it is so far up IMDb's top 250 is beyond me.

Review Title: Scarcely has a comedy provided its audience with so much insight, intelligence, and human affection
Review Text: "All is well," the three "idiot" heroes of 3 Idiots recite when they become frazzled or nervous, while frantically tapping their hearts. They know that reciting the oversimplifying phrase will not provide their problems with a solution, or even a catalyst, but it gives them the onset courage they desperately need at a time of uncertainty and despair. One character even tries to use the line after an amateurishly-given birth may have gone terribly wrong. It's times like that when the saying may not be so effective, even if its goal is not to provide certain relief.But all is well, as they so often say. Rajkumar Hirani's 3 Idiots, my introduction to the world of Bollywood cinema, is a comedy-drama unlike any one I've ever seen, long, but never drawn out, frantic, but never desperate, and emotional, but never manipulating. It provides us with three of the most likable characters of the last decade, and with insight to the culture of Indians and their parents. Rarely has a comedy had this big of a brain in its head and a canvas of opportunities in mind, while possessing a title not even fit for a farce.Our title characters are ambitious but stunted Farhan Qureshi (R. Madhavan), jittery, fearful Raju Rastogi (Sharman Joshi), and borderline prodigy Ranchhoddas "Rancho" Shamaldas Chhanchad (Aamir Khan), three close friends who attend the Imperial College of Engineering (ICE), one of India's most prestigious colleges where only the best are accepted. The young boys already are facing some of life's greatest challenges, all of them pursuing the field of engineering when the only one who seems to have a passion for the material is Rancho. Farhan was forced to become skilled in the field because of his strict father, and Raju is pursuing the field with the goal of getting his family out of poverty, with his father's expensive health bills, his mother's fragile core, and his sister's inability to marry all on the table. Rancho is studying because he feels that one should pursue passion and make a career out of it, so it doesn't feel like work. If you're passionate and devoted to your work, success will find you - yet this is only one of the countless morals and insights 3 Idiots provide its viewer with.The film chronicles their issues in college in flashback, with instances in the presence popping up to show Farhan and Raju searching for their pal Rancho, who has disappeared and avoided contact for several years now. This is not your typical flashback film, however. It uses this method much to its advantage, and doesn't feel like a narrative within a narrative. It seals both stories nicely, but it takes the viewer on a roller-coaster ride of emotions through carefully constructed events before it even arrives at those endings.One of their problems in college is the professor/dean of the campus, Viru Sahastrabuddhe (Boman Irani), who the three derogatorily call "Virus" when his back is turned. Virus is strict and often largely unsympathetic to the problems of his students, which allows the film to explore a dark topic one assumes a comedy wouldn't dare cross paths with and that is the issue of Indian teen suicide. One kid had his father have a stroke, rendering him unable to focus on academics for months, falling gravely behind. Virus is grossly unsympathetic to his case, denying him an extension on a project he's work so unrealistically hard on but can not perfect.If anything, we can say 3 Idiots is a message to the parents of the Indian youth and that is to stop applying pressure to an already stressed out demographic. I've read several articles articulating the point that Indian teenagers are among one of the highest in suicide rates, and that many parents put an unrealistic amount of pressure, duty, and responsibility on the child to the point of a physical and mental collapse. You're given the unalienable freedom to raise your kids with the method you choose, but not every method is perfect, and when your method is one of the ones that leads to suicide, mental health problems, and perpetuates fear and anxiety in the minds of teenagers, then you may want to reevaluate your methods.3 Idiots also provides us with the idea that many Indian children are told what to become in life early and are discouraged from exploration or even personal ambition. This I can't believe. What an unhappy, uninspiring, completely wasted life to slog through everyday doing something you loathe all because it was the lucky career choice of your parents. Yet I'm sure millions have been lead through this same life. It's the worst way of "living through a person" that I can imagine.The film runs ten minutes shy of three hours, which for few viewers may seem too long and uncomfortable. I was skeptical myself walking in rather blind. Almost every Bollywood film I've conducted research on seems to run an upwards of three hours, some extending and testing the waters to roughly five hours. If other Bollywood works have characters drawn along the same lines of being relatable, human, and easily accessible and not biased towards race or social class, I can't see it being a struggle to get through any of the industry's films.Through every quick-witted song and dance number, through every comedic scene, through every instance of dramatic despair, emotionally alive moment, solemnly poignant sequence, suspenseful setup, to a lovely, heartfelt conclusion, 3 Idiots is a wonderful, invaluable endeavor in the world of film. Its actors are beautifully fitting for their roles, assisted by a script so human and true to life that it is honestly hard to believe, and captured through beautifully alive cinematography to collectively give us a beautiful package not just in the figurative sense. This is arguably one of the best comedies I've ever seen.

Review Title: Scarcely has a comedy provided its audience with so much insight, intelligence, and human affection
Review Text: "All is well," the three "idiot" heroes of 3 Idiots recite when they become frazzled or nervous, while frantically tapping their hearts. They know that reciting the oversimplifying phrase will not provide their problems with a solution, or even a catalyst, but it gives them the onset courage they desperately need at a time of uncertainty and despair. One character even tries to use the line after an amateurishly-given birth may have gone terribly wrong. It's times like that when the saying may not be so effective, even if its goal is not to provide certain relief.But all is well, as they so often say. Rajkumar Hirani's 3 Idiots, my introduction to the world of Bollywood cinema, is a comedy-drama unlike any one I've ever seen, long, but never drawn out, frantic, but never desperate, and emotional, but never manipulating. It provides us with three of the most likable characters of the last decade, and with insight to the culture of Indians and their parents. Rarely has a comedy had this big of a brain in its head and a canvas of opportunities in mind, while possessing a title not even fit for a farce.Our title characters are ambitious but stunted Farhan Qureshi (R. Madhavan), jittery, fearful Raju Rastogi (Sharman Joshi), and borderline prodigy Ranchhoddas "Rancho" Shamaldas Chhanchad (Aamir Khan), three close friends who attend the Imperial College of Engineering (ICE), one of India's most prestigious colleges where only the best are accepted. The young boys already are facing some of life's greatest challenges, all of them pursuing the field of engineering when the only one who seems to have a passion for the material is Rancho. Farhan was forced to become skilled in the field because of his strict father, and Raju is pursuing the field with the goal of getting his family out of poverty, with his father's expensive health bills, his mother's fragile core, and his sister's inability to marry all on the table. Rancho is studying because he feels that one should pursue passion and make a career out of it, so it doesn't feel like work. If you're passionate and devoted to your work, success will find you - yet this is only one of the countless morals and insights 3 Idiots provide its viewer with.The film chronicles their issues in college in flashback, with instances in the presence popping up to show Farhan and Raju searching for their pal Rancho, who has disappeared and avoided contact for several years now. This is not your typical flashback film, however. It uses this method much to its advantage, and doesn't feel like a narrative within a narrative. It seals both stories nicely, but it takes the viewer on a roller-coaster ride of emotions through carefully constructed events before it even arrives at those endings.One of their problems in college is the professor/dean of the campus, Viru Sahastrabuddhe (Boman Irani), who the three derogatorily call "Virus" when his back is turned. Virus is strict and often largely unsympathetic to the problems of his students, which allows the film to explore a dark topic one assumes a comedy wouldn't dare cross paths with and that is the issue of Indian teen suicide. One kid had his father have a stroke, rendering him unable to focus on academics for months, falling gravely behind. Virus is grossly unsympathetic to his case, denying him an extension on a project he's work so unrealistically hard on but can not perfect.If anything, we can say 3 Idiots is a message to the parents of the Indian youth and that is to stop applying pressure to an already stressed out demographic. I've read several articles articulating the point that Indian teenagers are among one of the highest in suicide rates, and that many parents put an unrealistic amount of pressure, duty, and responsibility on the child to the point of a physical and mental collapse. You're given the unalienable freedom to raise your kids with the method you choose, but not every method is perfect, and when your method is one of the ones that leads to suicide, mental health problems, and perpetuates fear and anxiety in the minds of teenagers, then you may want to reevaluate your methods.3 Idiots also provides us with the idea that many Indian children are told what to become in life early and are discouraged from exploration or even personal ambition. This I can't believe. What an unhappy, uninspiring, completely wasted life to slog through everyday doing something you loathe all because it was the lucky career choice of your parents. Yet I'm sure millions have been lead through this same life. It's the worst way of "living through a person" that I can imagine.The film runs ten minutes shy of three hours, which for few viewers may seem too long and uncomfortable. I was skeptical myself walking in rather blind. Almost every Bollywood film I've conducted research on seems to run an upwards of three hours, some extending and testing the waters to roughly five hours. If other Bollywood works have characters drawn along the same lines of being relatable, human, and easily accessible and not biased towards race or social class, I can't see it being a struggle to get through any of the industry's films.Through every quick-witted song and dance number, through every comedic scene, through every instance of dramatic despair, emotionally alive moment, solemnly poignant sequence, suspenseful setup, to a lovely, heartfelt conclusion, 3 Idiots is a wonderful, invaluable endeavor in the world of film. Its actors are beautifully fitting for their roles, assisted by a script so human and true to life that it is honestly hard to believe, and captured through beautifully alive cinematography to collectively give us a beautiful package not just in the figurative sense. This is arguably one of the best comedies I've ever seen.

Review Title: This inspired me to watch more Bollywood movies
Review Text: Until recently my only exposure to Indian cinema was Satyajit Ray. Like a lot of Americans, my knowledge of Bollywood was based entirely on film clips and American take-offs of lavish musical numbers.I searched a few lists of top Bollywood films and settled on 3 Idiots, which turned out to be a wonderful decision. The movie is terrifically funny and engaging, seamlessly moving from comedy to tragedy to musical numbers.The story centers around a brilliant, iconoclastic technical student who spends the movie telling people to follow their dreams. While generally lighthearted, the movie strongly addresses the apparently terrible stresses on Indian college students. But in spite of some dark moments, overall this is about as feel-good as you can get. Feel-good to an absurd degree? Sure. But what's so bad about feeling good?When I think of Bollywood I think of big musical numbers, and there are a handful here, although they're a tiny part of the three-hour running time. Still, they are tremendous fun.To sum up, I saw a Bollywood movie, I laughed, I cried, and now I want to see more.UPDATE: I saw more. So many good movies out there!

Review Title: This Christmas & New Year, go gift yourselves - watch this phenomena (Not for Professors) !
Review Text: I'm an IITian myself and hence, needless to say, was looking forward to this movie as it is based on the life in IITs. I went with my family and watched the first-day-first-show and was I pleased ? In one sentence - The best Bollywood movie ever ! If you are into movies, then this one is not to be missed unless you are the 4th idiot. I cried, I laughed and I enjoyed every moment of the 3 hours that I spent watching this gem. The songs that seemed mediocre before watching the movie, feel like perfect for the movie. They are so ideal for the situations that I just loved them.As you can understand, they could not bring the name of any IIT into the movie to avoid disputes. But trust me, whatever they have shown about the life in IITs, it's all true. Right from suicides, cold-hearted Professors to lack of encouragement and support for talents who can really bring serious change in this world. The movie successfully depicts how once inside, your life is only about meaningless grades, go get into the race before it's too late.Take my free advice and avoid any reviews or words from people who have already seen the movie. Don't let anyone spoil any scene from the movie. Rush to the nearest theater and experience the phenomenon for yourself. You'll bless me for this advice !

Review Title: Aamir has yet again chased excellence & success follows him !
Review Text: Plot Two idiots Farhan (Madhavan) and Raju (Sharman) sets outs on a journey to find their lost friend and the 3rd idiot Rancho (Aamir).. As the journey starts off, their college life and old memories of their friendship, with a unique free-thinker Rancho who inspired them and changed their lives, are unfolded through flashbacks and followed with some twists and turns...My Views 3 Idiots looks at our educational system which pressurizes the students and does not allow to chose his destination by his own wish, in a satirical way and brings out some of the flaws in it... On parallel there is a love angle between Rancho and Pia (Kareena)... Many of the situations/characters have resemblance or inspiration to "Five Point Someone", blockbuster novel penned by Chetan Bhagat...Both humour and emotional quotients are amalgamated in the right proportion, full credits to Raju for that... Film offers lots of quality rib-tickling moments throughout.. The intermission point offers a twist that is totally out of the blue and keeps you hooked to the movie ! Just when you think this feel-good comic caper is leading to its culmination, there is another surprising element at the climax which is brilliantly weaved out by Hirani...Technically, it rocks with astounding and visually arresting cinematography, the aerial shots of Shimla and Ladakh are a pleasant treat for our eyes... Editing was good... Song visualizations were cool... Music was good and well placed with the flow of the narration...Coming to performances, all of them comes out with convincing portrayals, but it is Aamir Khan, the real showman of Bollywood, steals the shows with yet another stellar portrayal and will go down as one of his best performances. His looks and mannerisms as a college student was genuine and consummate.. Madhavan and Sharman adds up with commendable performances... Kareena does her part well... Among the supporting, Boman Irani as the eccentric head of the institution was too good...On the downside, there are some unbelievable filmy situations which can be excused as writer's liberty and the episode of Mona's delivery was a bit far stretched.. Still these minor negatives does not affect this wholesome entertainer ! In short the basic theme of the movie is that "chase excellence and success will follow".. Just like that Aamir and Raju has chased excellence and success is following them...So to sum up, Aamir and Hirani has delivered the best movie of 2009... Do yourself a favour by seeing this highly emotional and feel good comic entertainer...Verdict : THE BEST of 2009 ! Rating : 9/10for more reviews of the latest Indian movies, visit www.snehasallapam.com

Review Title: If this had come from Hollywood and not Bollywood it might have cleaned up at the Oscars.
Review Text: The title may conjur up images of the 3 Stooges and the crude humour of the opening might suggest a Hindi "Porky's" but with a running time of almost three hours "3 Idiots" clearly has its mind on higher things. It's a Bollywood buddy-movie devised as an epic and determined to outdo all the competition and being a Bollywood movie is full of large-scale musical numbers as well as a lot of very broad and very funny comedy.The 3 idiots of the title meet as engineering students, become life-long friends but are separated by that old fickle finger of fate. It's also bold enough to deal with such issues as suicide and poverty and, of course, the caste system and is lifted onto an altogether higher plain by a terrific performance from Aamir Khan as 'the idiot' the other two worship and who, naturally, isn't an idiot at all but the sharpest tool in the box and he's brilliantly supported by Sharman Joshi as his potentially suicidal friend and Omi Vaidya as his number one rival. Brilliantly directed by Rajkumar Hirani this is the kind of film Hollywood could learn a lot from and which, had it been a Hollywood film, might well have swept the boards come Oscar time.

Review Title: 3 idiots is a masterpiece and easily one of the greatest Bollywood movies of all time
Review Text: I saw the paid premiere of 3 idiots today.On the whole, 3 idiots is a winner all the way everythingwise.3 idiots suits the term 'landmark cinema' and has all it takes to help Bollywood take a giant step into world cinema with pride.3 idiots is a sparkling example of qualitative cinema.3 idiots not only entertains, it also enlightens.3 idiots is forward-thinking, but also makes you recall your roots. It promises lots of laughs and also a heartwarming message.There are movies aplenty, but very few remain etched in your memory and possess recall value. 3 idiots is one of those films. Indisputably and undeniably, this Aamir-Madhavan-Sharman outing is worth the price of the ticket and more.The film will set new records and has the merits to emerge one of the biggest hits of all times. The weekend business should be historic, the Week 1 business should be unparalleled, the lifetime gross should be amongst the biggest of all times. In short, 3 idiots has 'Blockbuster' written all over it.The story of 3 idiots has been told differently and most importantly, the story offers so many twists-n-turns that you just can't guess what would unfold next.There's so much happening in every scene and the screenplay is so gripping that you don't feel the need to look at the auditorium ceiling or at your watch at brief intervals. You aren't restless. 3 idiots demonstrates how strong film-making can enhance and elevate an already solid concept.An Aamir Khan film is nothing short of an event. The supremely talented actor acts in one film a year and no two films are ever identical in terms of plot line.To sum up, 3 idiots is commercial Hindi cinema at its best. The film has 'Hit' written all over it. Let me put it this way: Cancel whatever you're doing today and go watch 3 idiots instead.Director Rajkumar Hirani strikes a fine balance between humor and emotions. The comic portions are executed with panache, the drama is attention-grabbing and the emotional quotient is strong enough to turn you moist-eyed. The marriage of humor and emotions as also technique and content is what drives 3 idiots to the winning post.

Review Title: 3 hours of stereotypes, melodrama and rehashed jokes from the nineties
Review Text: I got to see this movie at a special screening before it hit the theaters. The high expectation was palpable. After all, Aamir Khan has been producing successive hits over the years. Now for the movie - which starts in an engineering college in India, goes on to show the lives of three of its students through the years till they graduate. This being a Bollywood film, obviously has a smattering of romance and song and dance sequences. Right from the start where Aamir jaywalks into the college building without a care in the world to the time he starts questioning the teaching methods in class, its just a matter of a couple of days. Some cheeky guy this is! Unfortunately, all his antics and funny lines are as old as the college building he is in. You would have heard these jokes a hundred times over. Anyways, that is the best part of the film since it at least elicits a few laughs. The rest of the 3 hours is filled with stereotypes and melodramatic scenes which are ridiculous at best. It seems that these days in India, anyone who can make the audience cry can laugh all the way to the bank. Aamir has this knowledge and isn't afraid to use it.
